以说它是基于人工智能技术发展而优化的一种语言模型。
      Also, the fact that ChatGPT is good at multi-tasking is a breakthrou

gh. 此外，ChatGPT擅长多任务处理，这无疑是一项突破。This is beyond the imagination of many people, because in the past AI models could perform only a single task. 这超出了许多人的想象，因为在过去，人工智能模型只能执行单一任务。For example, if you used an AI program to translate something, the program would just translate the piece. 例如，如果你使用一个AI程序来翻译某段文字，该程序只会翻译这段文字。Now with ChatGPT, multi-tasking is easy to achieve.现在有了ChatGPT，多任务处理变得轻而易举。
As a pre-trained language model, ChatGPT is equipped with a massive volume of data, so it can accurately decide which word comes next in a sentence and thus stabilize its reliable performance, especially in the era of deep neural networks.作为一款预训练语言模型，ChatGPT拥有海量数据，因此它能准确判断句子中下一个词是什么，从而保持其稳定可靠的性能，尤其是在深度神经网络时代。 That’s why many users are surprised at its ability to come up with human-like responses, creating the impression that it could replace humans in certain jobs one day.这就是为什么许多用户对其能够给出类似人类的回复感到惊讶，并认为它有朝一日可能会在某些工作中取代人类。
    Another feature of ChatGPT is that it has the ability to reinforce learning t

有朝一日可能会在某些工作中取代人类。
    Another feature of ChatGPT is that it has the ability to reinforce learning t

hrough feedback.ChatGPT的另一大特色是其通过反馈进行强化学习的能力。 It can recalculate figures or rewrite reports according to the user’s feedback on the answer. 它能根据用户对答案的反馈重新计算数据或重写报告。And this is one of the reasons why its performance is being optimized.这也是其性能不断优化的原因之一。
     Yet it still has its shortcomings. 然而，它仍存在不足。For one thing, it could only be applied in a limited number of fields as it is a content-generating model. 首先，由于它是一个内容生成模型，因此只能应用于有限数量的领域。And the content it generates depends on what it has been pre-trained in. 而且，它生成的内容取决于其预先训练的内容。Therefore, despite writing articles, essays, jokes and even poetry, ChatGPT could be used only as reference, just like a search engine.因此，尽管ChatGPT可以撰写文章、散文、笑话甚至诗歌，但它只能作为参考，就像搜索引擎一样。 It can never be a substitute for professional, in-depth knowledge ad research.它永远无法替代专业、深入的知识和研究。
Due to these limitations, investment in ChatGPT should focus on research and development, and investors should not expect quick returns on investment. 鉴于这些局限性，对ChatGPT的投资应聚焦于研发，投资者不应期望快速

获得投资回报。Yet as a promising AI development, ChatGPT is likely to advance in the medium, if not the short term.然而，作为一项有前景的人工智能开发项目，ChatGPT即使不能在短期内取得进展，也很有可能在中期内取得突破。
     China Needs Its Own ChatGPT Model中国需要自己的ChatGPT模型 Zhang Huaping, an associate professor on natural language processing at Beijing Institute of Technology.张华平，北京理工大学自然语言处理副教授。
     It is important to view ChatGPT objectively.客观看待ChatGPT很重要。 As a pre-trained, large-scale language model developed by OpenAI, ChatGPT is a kind of groundbreaking research.作为OpenAI开发的一种预训练的大型语言模型，ChatGPT是一项具有开创性的研究。 Large-scale language models may have an impact on copywriting and other industries, and will also change the modes of education and scientific research. 大型语言模型可能会对文案撰写等行业产生影响，也将改变教育和科研的模式。In the future, some simple, repetitive and less innovative jobs, and even some white-collar jobs, may get replaced by large-scale language models.未来，一些简单、重复性较强且创新性较低的工作，甚至一些白领工作，都可能被大型语言模型所取代。 But it is not infallible, and its potential risks have n

一些简单、重复性较强且创新性较低的工作，甚至一些白领工作，都可能被大型语言模型所取代。 But it is not infallible, and its potential risks have n

ot been thoroughly studied.但ChatGPT并非绝对可靠，其潜在风险尚未得到深入研究。 You can talk to it, acquire general knowledge or even get entertained by it, but it cannot be the sole source of your information, let alone take your place.你可以与它对话，获取一般知识，甚至从中获得娱乐，但它不能成为你信息的唯一来源，更不能取代你。
It is an AI program. And although its functions are very helpful to humans, they should not be deified. 它是一个人工智能程序。尽管其功能对人类非常有帮助，但我们不应将其神化。First, AI itself has been on the cusp of rising to greater heights in recent years. 首先，近年来，人工智能本身已处于更上一层楼的风口浪尖。What’s more, ChatGPT can meet some “lazy” people’s need of avoiding repetitive and boring mental work. 此外，ChatGPT能够满足一些“懒惰”之人避免重复且枯燥脑力劳动的需求。But mental work itself is part of the human cognitive faculty.但脑力劳动本身是人类认知能力的一部分。
   Search engines used to retrieve large volumes of information, enabling people to view page after page. 搜索引擎曾用于检索大量信息，使人们能够一页接一页地浏览。With the aid of ChatGPT, search engines can present more precise answers to complex questions, and users can now get information in a

more relaxing way of dialogue.借助ChatGPT，搜索引擎能够为复杂问题提供更精确的答案，用户现在可以通过更轻松的对话方式获取信息。
     China has also developed its own large-scale language models, such as Huawei’s Pangu NLP models, Baidu’s ERNIE, Tencent’s HUNYUAN NLP models, and Alibaba’s AliceMind, and the performance of the models in natural language processing has been excellent.中国也开发了自己的大型语言模型，如华为的盘古自然语言处理（NLP）模型、百度的ERNIE、腾讯的浑元自然语言处理模型以及阿里巴巴的AliceMind，这些模型在自然语言处理方面的表现非常出色。 But China’s large-scale language models need to focus more on deeper technologies, and should not blindly follow trends.但中国的大型语言模型需要更加关注更深层次的技术，不应盲目跟风。
     Since ChatGPT is also a dialogue model, China needs more custom models in specific fields, such as modern Chinese language, ancient Chinese language, minority languages, and dialects, to break language barriers among people.
     由于ChatGPT也是一个对话模型，中国需要在特定领域（如现代汉语、古汉语、少数民族语言和方言）开发更多定制模型，以打破人与人之间的语言障碍。

barriers among people.
     由于ChatGPT也是一个对话模型，中国需要在特定领域（如现代汉语、古汉语、少数民族语言和方言）开发更多定制模型，以打破人与人之间的语言障碍。

Unit Five Smart  Manufacturing智能制造 Text A Smart Manufacturing: The Future of Making Is Digital智能制造：制造业的未来是数字化 The technologies of the Fourth Industrial Revolution (Industry 4.0)—connected robotics, 3D printing, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things—made smart manufacturing possible and sped innovation to a dizzying clip.第四次工业革命（工业4.0）的技术——互联机器人、3D打印、云计算、人工智能和物联网——使智能制造成为可能，并极大地加速了创新进程，使其达到令人目眩的速度。
The dawn of mechanical manufacturing happened during the First Industrial Revolution at about the end of the 18th century, powered by water and steam. 机械制造业的开端发生在18世纪末的第一次工业革命期间，其动力源于水和蒸汽。Two centuries later, electricity enabled the Second Industrial Revolution’s mass-production assembly lines and division of labor. 两个世纪后，电力推动了第二次工业革命中大规模生产流水线和劳动分工的出现。Rapid progress led to the Third Industrial Revolution around 1970, when computers and information technology automated some industrial processes and led to more sophisticated communication.1970年左右，随着计算机和信息技术的快速发展，第

三次工业革命应运而生，这些技术使一些工业流程实现了自动化，并带来了更先进的通信方式。
Since the term Industry 4.0 was coined（create，invent） in 2011, its associated technologies have developed to allow a data-rich, interconnected, and highly automated form of production called smart manufacturing.自2011年“工业4.0”这一术语被提出以来（一词问世以来），与其相关技术不断发展，催生了一种数据丰富、互联互通且高度自动化的生产形式，即智能制造。
What Is Smart Manufacturing?什么是智能制造？
     Smart manufacturing refers to the widespread digitization of all manufacturing practices, from the factory floor to all aspects of business. It includes product design, supply chain, production, distribution, and sales.智能制造指的是从工厂车间到企业运营各个环节的所有生产流程实现全面数字化。涵盖产品设计、供应链、生产、分销以及销售。
Smart manufacturing leverages Industry 4.0, which is characterized by interconnected cyber-physical systems such as intelligent robots and machines that can self-diagnose and warn of possible failures.智能制造借助了工业4.0技术这一概念，其特点是具备互联的网联物理系统，如具备自我诊断功能和能够发出可能故障警报的智能机器人和机械设备。
The proliferating IoT brings more powerful devices and machines with smart sensors

能故障警报的智能机器人和机械设备。
The proliferating IoT brings more powerful devices and machines with smart sensors

that upload continuous streams of usage data to the cloud for analysis. 物联网的迅速发展带来了更多功能强大的设备和机器，这些设备和机器配备了智能传感器，能够持续将使用数据流上传至云端进行分析。
Those Big Data sets are crunched using AI with machine learning that becomes more accurate and predictive as it absorbs more data.这些大数据集通过人工智能和机器学习进行处理，随着吸收更多数据，其准确性和预测性不断提高。
Automation and data connectivity create more agile supply chains, in which ships and trucks “talk” to warehouses, autonomous or semi-autonomous vehicles, and drones. 自动化和数据连接创造了更敏捷的供应链，其中船舶和卡车能够与仓库、自动驾驶或半自动驾驶车辆以及无人机进行“对话”。Mobile robots and cobots (collaborative robots) are also folded into this process, making shipping and logistics more automated.移动机器人和协作机器人（cobots）也被纳入这一流程，使航运和物流更加自动化。
Smart manufacturing helps manufacturers become more efficient, stay ahead of the competitive curve, and explore new business models and practices.智能制造助力制造商提升效率，保持竞争优势，并探索新的商业模式和实践方法。Benefits of Adopting Smart Manufacturing 采用智能制造的好处     Businesses can adopt smart manufacturing to help streamline proc

esses, increase productivity, stay competitive, and prepare for the future—including for unprecedented events, such as a pandemic.企业可以采用智能制造来帮助简化流程、提高生产力、保持竞争力，并为未来做好准备——包括应对前所未有的事件，如大流行病。
An ongoing poll from the nonprofit Manufacturing Enterprise Solutions Association (MESA) asks companies what is the main challenge that is slowing their progress toward smart manufacturing.非营利组织制造企业解决方案协会（MESA）正在进行的一项民意调查询问企业，阻碍其向智能制造转型的主要挑战是什么。
About 58% cited financial concerns or lack of knowledge about the related technologies. To be sure, adopting smart manufacturing requires significant investments of financial and human resources and involves some risk.约58%的企业表示，主要原因是财务方面的担忧或对相关技术缺乏了解。诚然，采用智能制造需要大量财力和人力资源的投资，并存在一定的风险。
However, the 2019 “Smart Factory Study” from Deloitte and the Manufacturers Alliance concludes that any manufacturer, whether it has begun smart-factory initiatives or not, can derive value for its business by doing so—and that the value of those initiatives generally outweighs t

ive value for its business by doing so—and that the value of those initiatives generally outweighs t

he financial and operational risks.然而，德勤和制造商联盟2019年的“智能工厂研究”得出结论，任何制造商，无论是否已开始实施智能工厂计划，都可以通过这样做为其业务带来价值，而这些计划的价值通常大于财务和运营风险。
The study broke respondents into two groups: Group A (49% of companies) that had no ongoing smart-factory initiatives, and Group B (51%) that had some level of ongoing smart-factory initiatives. 该研究将受访者分为两组：A组（占公司总数的49%）没有正在进行的智能工厂计划，B组（占51%）有一定程度的正在进行的智能工厂计划。The study determined that Group A’s productivity index for 2015–2018 likely declined 2.3% per year while Group B saw average annual productivity rise 3.3% during the same period.该研究确定，2015年至2018年期间，A组的生产率指数可能每年下降2.3%，而同期B组的平均年生产率则上升了3.3%。Furthermore, the Group B companies experienced a 10% average increase in production output and an 11% average increase in factory capacity utilization.此外，B组公司的生产产量平均增长了10%，工厂产能利用率平均增长了11%。
Most of the companies not yet implementing smart manufacturing still saw its importance. 大多数尚未实施智能制造的公司仍然认识到了其重要性。In the study, 86% of manufacturers surveyed believe that within five years

大多数尚未实施智能制造的公司仍然认识到了其重要性。In the study, 86% of manufacturers surveyed believe that within five years

, smart-manufacturing solutions will be the main driver of competitiveness, which highlights how important smart-manufacturing capabilities will be to enable manufacturers to be competitive in the coming years. 在这项研究中，86%的受访制造商认为，在未来五年内，智能制造解决方案将成为竞争力的主要驱动力，这凸显了智能制造能力对于制造商在未来几年保持竞争力的重要性。It is only a matter of time before all manufacturers will have to adopt smart-manufacturing technologies.所有制造商都将不得不采用智能制造技术，这只是一个时间问题。
How to Start the Smart-Manufacturing Journey?如何开启智能制造之旅？
    For businesses interested in smart manufacturing but intimidated by financial or technical barriers, the good news is that they do not have to transition factories in one big leap.对于那些对智能制造感兴趣但因财务或技术障碍而望而却步的企业而言，好消息是它们不必一次性对工厂进行大规模改造。/好消息是它们无需一步到位地完成工厂转型。In fact, the “Smart Factory Study” shows that small steps often lead to big wins.事实上，“智能工厂研究”表明，小步快走往往能带来大收获。 Successful companies from the study usually started by securing support from executives in the C-suite—especially the chief technology officer—and laun

by securing support from executives in the C-suite—especially the chief technology officer—and laun

ching multiple small projects with low initial investments. 研究中那些成功的公司通常首先会获得高管（尤其是首席技术官）的支持，并启动多个初期投资较低的小项目。By tying those projects to measurable business metrics, they could use any early successes as an incentive to seek additional incremental investments.通过将这些项目与可衡量的业务指标联系起来，它们可以利用任何早期成功作为激励，寻求更多的增量投资。
It is suggested that the manufacturers starting out on a smart-manufacturing journey should begin by making their analog data digital: Eliminate （to remove or get rid of sth/sb排除；清除；消除） manual analog processes, and find a system that captures everything in one digital database. 建议那些刚刚踏上智能制造之旅的制造商，首先应将他们的模拟数据数字化：消除手动模拟流程，并找到一个能将所有内容捕获到一个数字数据库中的系统。
That’s the foundational data on which to build feedback loops by inputting more information from factory-floor processes. 这是通过输入更多来自工厂车间流程的信息来构建反馈回路的基础数据。This additional input could come from sensor data, but it could also be from workers inputting data at different points in the process.这些额外的输入可能来自传感器数据，但也可能来自工人在流程中不同环节输入的数据。
Once manufactu

rers’ problems are revealed by data, they can start thinking about making changes on multiple dimensions—that’s where introducing more computation and algorithms can help. 一旦制造商的问题通过数据被揭示出来，他们就可以开始考虑在多个维度上进行变革——这就是引入更多计算和算法能提供帮助的地方。
“You let the algorithms give you different exploration possibilities so you can figure out the best path forward,” Jonnalagadda, Autodesk vice president of go-to-market strategy and marketing for Design and Manufacturing, says. “你让算法为你提供不同的探索可能性，这样你就能找出最佳的前进道路，”欧特克公司负责设计与制造领域市场战略与营销的副总裁乔纳拉加达说。“Now, you have a digital backbone. You have input and feedback loops giving you insights and the help of algorithms that give you options for continuing to refine and optimize factory-floor operations.”“现在，你拥有了一个数字中枢。输入和反馈循环为你提供洞察力，而算法则帮助你继续完善和优化工厂车间的运营。”
   Impact of Smart Manufacturing 智能制造的影响    Smart manufacturing can help a business become much more efficient with its resources, enhance worker safety, and facilitate worker training.  智能制造可以帮助企业大幅提高资源利用效率，增强工人安全，并促

ts resources, enhance worker safety, and facilitate worker training.  智能制造可以帮助企业大幅提高资源利用效率，增强工人安全，并促

进工人培训。It can also make a business more agile, according to MESA Knowledge Committee Chair Khris Kammer. 据制造企业解决方案协会（Manufacturing Enterprise Solutions Association，简称MESA）知识委员会主席克里斯·卡默（Khris Kammer）表示，智能制造还能使企业更加灵活。
“Look at the number of companies during the pandemic that pivoted to manufacturing other things, whether personal protective equipment (PPE) or related medical devices,” Kammer says, “they could do that only because they had the agility to introduce a new product into their manufacturing environment very quickly and retool, retrain, and execute in a very rapid fashion./way”“看看疫情期间有多少公司转而生产其他产品，无论是个人防护装备（PPE）还是相关医疗设备，”卡默说，“他们之所以能做到这一点，只是因为他们具备迅速将新产品引入制造环境、快速重组、快速再培训和快速执行的灵活性。”
Smart-manufacturing firms can be agile with their products and participate in the “mass customization (MC)” trend that lets manufacturers deliver low-volume, high-variability products. 智能制造企业能够灵活调整其产品，并顺应“大规模定制（MC）”的趋势，该趋势使制造商能够提供小批量、高可变性的产品。Smart manufacturing has the potential to improve outcomes for

individual businesses and the manufacturing industry as a whole.智能制造有潜力改善单个企业和整个制造业的业绩。
   Another new business model that smart manufacturing has enabled, manufacturing as a service (MaaS), could help make the supply chain more resilient to disruptions like those in 2020. 智能制造所催生的另一种新型商业模式——制造即服务（MaaS），可能有助于使供应链更能抵御像2020年那样的中断。
Just as ride-share services rely on machine learning to match drivers with passengers very quickly, MaaS requires machine learning to quickly and effectively match manufacturers with customers.正如共享出行服务/网约车服务依靠机器学习来快速匹配司机和乘客一样，MaaS也需要机器学习来快速有效地匹配制造商和客户。
   The “Accelerating Smart Manufacturing” report focuses on another development with industry-wide implications: the “network effect” benefits of smart-manufacturing ecosystems, wherein many companies and entities meaningfully work together using advanced, connected technologies to meet/fulfil common objectives.《加速智能制造》报告聚焦于另一项具有行业广泛影响的发展：智能制造生态系统的“网络效应”优势，即众多公司和实体利用先进、互联的技术进行有意义的合作，以实现共同目标。
   Smart-manufacturin

智能制造》报告聚焦于另一项具有行业广泛影响的发展：智能制造生态系统的“网络效应”优势，即众多公司和实体利用先进、互联的技术进行有意义的合作，以实现共同目标。
   Smart-manufacturin

g ecosystems are mutually beneficial in any circumstance because they share access to collective wisdom and innovation.智能制造生态系统在任何情况下都是互惠互利的，因为它们能够共享集体智慧和创新资源。The report found that Fortune 500 manufacturers with more than 15 strategic alliances doubled year-over-year revenue growth in 2019 compared to those with fewer alliances. 报告发现，与战略联盟较少的《财富》500强制造商相比，拥有超过15个战略联盟的制造商在2019年的收入同比增长翻了一番。
Companies within smart-manufacturing ecosystems also experienced a faster pace of introducing products and services, expanded innovation capacity, faster maturity of their digital technology, and greater efficiencies that reduced operational costs.智能制造生态系统内的公司还加快了产品和服务的推出速度，扩大了创新能力，数字技术成熟度更快，效率更高，从而降低了运营成本。
    Smart Manufacturing’s Impact on Sustainability智能制造对可持续性的影响 Some smart-manufacturing technologies inherently promote efficient resource use. 一些智能制造技术本身就促进了资源的有效利用。Simulation software, for instance, can drastically reduce the amount of physical waste by moving many physical tests (such as vehicul

ar crash tests) into simulated environments.例如，仿真软件可以通过将许多物理测试（如车辆碰撞测试）转移到模拟环境中，大幅减少物理浪费。 Simulation can also predict the durability and lifespan of different materials and test alternate materials for end products with the lowest impact on the planet. 仿真还可以预测不同材料的耐用性和使用寿命，并测试替代材料，以生产对地球影响最小的最终产品。
Design for Manufacturing (DFM) can also introduce sustainability benefits into a product. For example, generative design software uses AI to generate an abundance of design options that can reduce product weight and the amount of material required while maintaining strength and cost.
   面向制造的设计（DFM）也可以为产品带来可持续性优势。例如，生成设计软件利用人工智能生成大量设计选项，这些选项可以在保持强度和成本的同时，减少产品重量和所需材料量。
With the right intention, smart manufacturing can actively support sustainability goals. 本着正确的初衷，智能制造能够积极助力实现可持续发展目标。
The Smart Factory@Wichita (a joint effort from Wichita State University and Deloitte) will include a full-scale production line demonstrating how to join existing manufacturing assets with smart-manufacturing tec

production line demonstrating how to join existing manufacturing assets with smart-manufacturing tec

Unit Seven  Smart Transportation Text A Five Future Transportation Technologies That Will Actually Happen未来将真正实现的五大交通技术 Over the next decade, the idea of getting to work on time, heading out to the hinterlands for your family vacation or even going to the game will become much easier.在未来十年，准时上班、前往偏远地区度假甚至去看比赛都将变得更加容易。Trains will use new magnetic rail systems. 火车将使用新型磁悬浮轨道系统。And an amazing new “hyperloop” train will speed along at 800 miles per hour.而令人惊叹的新型“超级高铁/超高速列车”将以每小时800英里的速度疾驰。
    The best part? These innovations are not just spinning their wheels. They are set to debut within the next 10 years or have already started transporting us.最棒的是？这些创新并非只是空谈。它们将在未来10年内首次亮相，或者已经开始为我们提供运输服务了。
“New technologies have the potential to make our roads and transit systems safer, greener and more efficient,” said Gregory Winfree, the administrator of the Department of Transportation’s Research and Innovative Technology Administration. “We are working hard to ensure that these technologies can be integrated safely into our existing system.”“新技术有潜力让我们的道路和交通系统变得更安全、更环保、更高效，”美国交通部研究与创新技术管理局局长格雷戈里·温弗里说。“我们正在努力确保这些技术能够安全地融入我们的现有系统。”
“We will need to do something,” said Thilo Koslowski, the lead automotive analyst at research firm Gartner, who studies next-generation transportation, “given that we will continue to see more vehicles on the road but won’t be able to grow infrastructure at the same time. We have to get smarter/wiser about using that infrastructure and/or innovate in passenger vehicles and mobility.”“鉴于我们将继续看到道路上出现更多车辆，但基础设施却无法同时增长，我们需要采取一些措施，”研究公司高德纳（Gartner）的首席汽车分析师蒂洛·科斯洛夫斯基（Thilo Koslowski）表示，“我们必须更明智地利用现有基础设施，或者在乘用车和出行方式上进行创新。”科斯洛夫斯基专门研究下一代交通工具。

Thilo Koslowski）表示，“我们必须更明智地利用现有基础设施，或者在乘用车和出行方式上进行创新。”科斯洛夫斯基专门研究下一代交通工具。

1. Hyperloop1. 超级高铁/超高速列车

One of the most exciting innovations in transportation has to be the hyperloop train. 交通领域最令人兴奋的创新之一非超级高铁莫属。Rising on nearly airless tubes at 800 mph, the train will transport you from LA to San Francisco in just 30 minutes.这种列车在近乎真空的管道中以800英里/小时的速度飞驰，仅需30分钟即可将你从洛杉矶送至旧金山。 Elon Musk announced a design scheme, but we have learned the concept is more than a pipe dream—it is now a real technology in development.埃隆·马斯克曾公布过其设计方案，但我们了解到，这一概念绝非空想——它现已成为一项正在开发中的真实技术。
“We’re moving toward conceptual design,” said Dr. Patricia Galloway, the co-leader of Hyperloop Transportation Technologies Inc., hinting at more than just a rough design sketch and an actual concept, something that is concrete and verifiable in the near future.“我们正在向概念设计迈进，”超级高铁运输技术股份有限公司（Hyperloop Transportation Technologies Inc.）的联合负责人帕特里夏·加洛韦（Patricia Galloway）博士说道，她暗示这不仅仅是一个粗略的设计草图和一个实际的概念，而是一个在不久的将来可以具体实现和验证的东西。
  “On paper, hyperloop is both cheaper and quieter, and it is potentially much faster, than a maglev train,” said Rob Enderle, an analyst with Enderle Group who studies Silicon Valley technology.“从理论上讲，与磁悬浮列车相比，超级高铁成本更低、噪音更小，且速度可能更快，”恩德勒集团（Enderle Group）研究硅谷技术的分析师罗布·恩德勒（Rob Enderle）表示。

2. Maglev Trains 磁悬浮列车

，超级高铁成本更低、噪音更小，且速度可能更快，”恩德勒集团（Enderle Group）研究硅谷技术的分析师罗布·恩德勒（Rob Enderle）表示。

2. Maglev Trains 磁悬浮列车

“Magnetic levitation” trains are not just a lofty dream held over from the 1950s. They are already in operation in Shanghai and Japan. South Korea is building a maglev train that will operate within the Incheon Airport, and China reportedly has a second maglev train in development.
“磁悬浮”列车并非20世纪50年代遗留下来的空想。它们已在上海和日本投入运营。韩国正在建造一条将在仁川机场内部运行的磁悬浮列车，据报道，中国还有第二条磁悬浮列车正在开发中。
A magnetic force lifts and propels (提升和推进)the train using a minimal amount of energy compared to diesel-powered or electric-powered trains. The trains whisk passengers along at up to 310 miles per hour. A planned maglev train will transport passengers over 200 miles between Nagoya and Tokyo in just 40 minutes, helping to free congested roads, reduce air pollution, and reduce accidents.与柴油动力或电力驱动的列车相比，磁力列车使用最少的能量即可实现列车的提升和推进。列车以高达每小时310英里的速度载着乘客疾驰而过。计划中的磁浮列车将在短短40分钟内将乘客从名古屋运送到东京，全程超过200英里，这将有助于缓解道路拥堵，减少空气污染，并降低事故发生率。
   Of course, the main issue with maglev trains is the high cost of development. Because of the fast speeds, the trains have to be routed directly between destinations.当然，磁悬浮列车的主要问题是开发成本高昂。由于速度极快，列车必须直接在目的地之间运行。

3. Autonomous Vehicles 自动驾驶汽车

A robotic driver can think faster and smarter than a human driver—and look in all directions at once. That’s the idea behind autonomous driving, where you take your hands off the wheel and let the car do the driving for you. Ford has already announced a project called Traffic Jam Assist and Cadillac is working on something called Super Cruise that lets the car take over.(get control of it) 机器人驾驶员/司机能比人类驾驶员/司机反应更快、更智能，还能同时观察各个方向。这就是自动驾驶背后的理念，让你无需手握方向盘，让汽车为你驾驶。福特已经宣布了一个名为“交通拥堵辅助”的项目，而凯迪拉克正在研发一种名为“超级巡航”的技术，可以让汽车接管驾驶任务。
     Still, Google is leading the charge. It now uses a fleet of about 24 Lexus RX450h vehicles that have logged a total of about 500,000 miles on California roads. The cars can look for exit ramps, detect buildings, stop suddenly for other cars and change speeds as needed.尽管如此，谷歌正在引领潮流。目前，谷歌拥有约24辆雷克萨斯RX450h汽车，这些汽车在加利福尼亚州的道路上总共行驶了约50万英里。这些汽车能够寻找出口匝道、识别建筑物、为其他车辆突然刹车，并根据需要改变速度。
      Enderle says there are many prototypes already on the road, especially those being tested in San Francisco. Nevada has already created laws that make them legal to use in cities, including Las Vegas. In fact, Enderle says autonomous driving could appear within two years if it weren’t for some nagging legal issues (such as how to insure them) and public safety concerns.恩德勒表示，目前已有许多原型车在道路上行驶，尤其是在旧金山进行测试的车辆。内华达州已制定法律，规定这些车辆在城市（包括拉斯维加斯）合法使用。事实上，恩德勒表示，如果不是因为一些棘手的法律问题（如如何为它们投保）和公共安全问题，自动驾驶汽车可能会在两年内问世。
Smart Cars智能汽车 One way to solve transportation problems in major cities is to make the cars much smaller and smarter. So-called “smart cars” have been around for many years. But there are signs of progress. Many automakers, including BMW and Nissan, already offer compact electric cars. The BMW i3, already available in Europe, can brake automatically when you take your foot off the accelerator, consumes no gasoline and operates for 80–100 miles per charge.
解决大城市交通问题的一种方法是让汽车变得更小、更智能。所谓的“智能汽车”已经存在多年。但有迹象表明，相关技术正在取得进展。包括宝马和日产在内的许多汽车制造商已经推出了紧凑型电动汽车。宝马i3已在欧洲上市，当你松开油门时，它会自动刹车，无需消耗汽油，每次充电可行驶80至100英里。
   “I do believe that there is a growing opportunity for new types of vehicles specifically designed for urban areas,” Koslowski said, adding that these cars need more of a “wow” factor and will have to become part of an urban area’s overall plan for better transportation in a city, not just showy small cars for individual drivers.科斯洛夫斯基（Koslowski）表示：“我确实认为，专为城市地区设计的新型车辆将有越来越多的机会。”他补充说，这些汽车需要更具“惊艳”的特质，并且必须成为城市整体交通规划的一部分，以改善城市交通状况，而不仅仅是供个人驾驶的炫酷小型汽车。

：“我确实认为，专为城市地区设计的新型车辆将有越来越多的机会。”他补充说，这些汽车需要更具“惊艳”的特质，并且必须成为城市整体交通规划的一部分，以改善城市交通状况，而不仅仅是供个人驾驶的炫酷小型汽车。

5. Urban Transport Pods 城市交通舱

What if you could jump into a moving pod and speed away to another part of the city? That is what the Milton Keynes neighborhood about 45 miles northwest of London is planning. The pods seat one person and move on their own over a pre-described route.如果可以跳进一个移动舱，快速前往城市的另一个区域，会怎样？位于伦敦西北约45英里处的米尔顿·凯恩斯社区正计划实现这一设想。这种舱体仅容纳一人，可沿预设路线自动行驶。
The idea is that the human operator interacts with the pod using a touchscreen in the windshield. You swipe to select a destination, and you can read the daily news, check your e-mail or even play a video game during the trip. There will be a built-in wireless hotspot to connect your gadgets. The pod operates on its own, showing its current route.其理念是，乘客通过挡风玻璃上的触摸屏与舱体互动：轻滑选择目的地，旅途中可阅读每日新闻、查看邮件甚至玩电子游戏。舱内配备内置无线热点，供电子设备连接。舱体完全自动运行，实时显示当前路线。
   Similar pods are already being used in Masdar City in Abu Dhabi and at the London Heathrow Airport, but both are used in tightly controlled areas.类似舱体已在阿布扎比的马斯达尔城和伦敦希思罗机场投入使用，但均限于严格管控的区域。
Jon Beasley, the program director

tightly controlled areas.类似舱体已在阿布扎比的马斯达尔城和伦敦希思罗机场投入使用，但均限于严格管控的区域。
Jon Beasley, the program director

at Transport Systems Catapult who is charged with developing the technology, says the project is an “urban laboratory” where they can test not just the autonomous pods but also how they work in a real public setting.负责技术开发的“交通系统弹射器”项目主管乔恩·比斯利表示，该项目是一个“城市实验室”，不仅可以测试自动驾驶舱体，还能观察其在真实公共场景中的运行情况。

Unit Eight Smart Farming 智慧农业 Text A Smart Farming: The Future of Agriculture “Smart farming”is an emerging concept that refers to managing farms using technologies like IoT, robotics, drones and AI to increase the quantity and quality of products while optimizing the human labor required by production.智能农业：农业的未来 “智能农业”是一个新兴概念，指的是利用物联网、机器人技术、无人机和人工智能等技术来管理农场，以提高产品的数量和质量，同时优化生产所需的人力。
The Internet of Things has provided ways to improve nearly every industry imaginable. In agric

需的人力。
The Internet of Things has provided ways to improve nearly every industry imaginable. In agric

ulture, IoT has not only provided solutions to often time-consuming and tedious tasks but is totally changing the way we think about agriculture. What exactly is a smart farm, though? Here is a rundown of what smart farming is and how it’s changing agriculture.物联网为几乎所有能想到的行业提供了改进途径。在农业领域，物联网不仅为那些往往耗时且繁琐的任务提供了解决方案，而且正在彻底改变我们对农业的看法。那么，智能农场究竟是什么呢？下面简要介绍一下智能农业及其对农业的变革。
What Is a Smart Farm?
      A smart farming refers to managing farms using modern information and communication technologies to increase the quantity and quality of products while optimizing the human labor required.什么是智能农场？
      智慧农业是指利用现代信息和通信技术来管理农场，以提高产品的数量和质量，同时优化所需的人力劳动。
Among the technologies available for present-day farmers are:当今农民可用的技术包括：
● Sensors: soil, water, light, humidity, temperature management传感器：土壤、水分、光照、湿度、温度管理 ● Software:  specialized software solutions that target specific farm types or application-agnostic IoT platforms 软件：针对特定农场类型或与应用程序无关的物联网平台的专用软件解决方案 ● Connectivity: cellular, LoRa 连接性：蜂窝式网络系统、低功耗

局域网无线标准 ● Location: GPS, satellites 位置：GPS，卫星 ● Robotics: autonomous tractors, processing facilities机器人技术：自动驾驶拖拉机、加工设施 ● Data analytics: standalone analytics solutions, data pipelines for downstream solutions数据分析：独立的分析解决方案、下游解决方案的数据管道 Armed with such tools, farmers can monitor field conditions and make strategic decisions for the whole farm or a single plant without even needing to step foot in the field.有了这些工具，农民无需亲临田间/踏入田地，就能监测田间状况，并为整个农场或单株植物做出战略决策。
The driving force of smart farming is IoT —connecting machines and sensors integrated on farms to make farming processes data-driven and automated.智能农业的驱动力是物联网——将农场中集成的机器和传感器连接起来，使农业流程数据化并实现自动化。
The IoT-Based Smart Farming Cycle基于物联网的智能农业循环 The core of IoT is the data you can draw from things and transmit over the Internet. To optimize the farming process, IoT devices installed on a farm should collect and process data in a repetitive cycle that enables farmers to react quickly to emerging issues and changes in ambient conditions. Smart

le that enables farmers to react quickly to emerging issues and changes in ambient conditions. Smart

farming follows a cycle like this one:物联网的核心在于，你可以从各种设备中提取数据，并通过互联网进行传输。为了优化农业流程，安装在农场上的物联网设备应在一个重复的周期内收集和处理数据，使农民能够快速应对新出现的问题和环境条件的变化。智能农业遵循这样一个循环周期：

1. Observation. Sensors record observational data from crops, livestock, soil, or the atmosphere.观测。传感器记录来自作物、牲畜、土壤或大气的观测数据。
2. Diagnostics. The sensor values are fed to a cloud-hosted IoT platform with predefined decision rules and models—also called “business logic”—that ascertain the condition of the examined object and identify any deficiencies or needs. 诊断。传感器值被输入到云端托管的物联网平台，该平台配备有预定义的决策规则和模型（也称为“业务逻辑”），用于确定被检查对象的状况，并识别任何缺陷或需求。
3. Decisions. After issues are revealed, the user, and/or machine learning-driven components of the IoT platform determine whether location-specific treatment is necessary and if so, which. 决策。问题暴露后，用户和/或物联网平台中的机器学习驱动组件将判断是否需要针对性地进行处理，若需要，则确定具体处理方式。
4. Action. After end-user evaluation and action, the cycle repeats from the beginning.行动。在最终用户评估和采取行动后，循环从头开始重复。

IoT Solutions to Agricultural Problems物联网解决农业问题 Many believe that IoT can add value to all areas of farming, from growing crops to forestry. While there are several ways that IoT can improve farming, two of the major ways IoT can revolutionize agriculture are precision farming and farming automation.许多人认为，物联网（IoT）可以为农业的各个领域，从种植作物到林业，增添价值。虽然物联网可以通过多种方式改善农业，但其中两大能够彻底改变农业的方式是精准农业和农业自动化。

1. Precision Farming1. 精准农业

可以为农业的各个领域，从种植作物到林业，增添价值。虽然物联网可以通过多种方式改善农业，但其中两大能够彻底改变农业的方式是精准农业和农业自动化。

1. Precision Farming1. 精准农业

Precision farming, or precision agriculture, is an umbrella concept for IoT-based approaches that make farming more controlled and accurate. In simple words, plants and cattle get precisely the treatment they need, determined by machines with superhuman accuracy. The biggest difference from the classical approach is that precision farming allows decisions to be made per square meter or even per plant/animal rather than for a field.精准农业是基于物联网的方法的总括性概念，这些方法使农业更具可控性和精确性。简而言之，植物和牲畜会得到由具有超人精度的机器所确定的精准的照疗。与传统方法最大的不同在于，精准农业允许根据每平方米甚至每株植物/每只动物的情况来做出决策，而不是针对整个田地。
By precisely measuring variations within a field, farmers can boost the effectiveness of pesticides and fertilizers, or use them selectively.通过精确测量田地内的变化，农民可以提高农药和化肥的效用，或选择性地使用它们。As is the case of precision agriculture, smart farming techniques enable farmers to better monitor the needs of individual animals and to adjust their nutrition accordingly, thereby preventing disease and enhancing herd health.与精准农业一样，智能养殖技术使农民能够更好地监测每只动物的需求，并据此调整其营养摄入，从而预防疾病并提升畜群健康。
Large farm owners can use wireless IoT applications to monitor the location, well-being, and health of their cattle. With this information, they can identify sick animals, so that they can be separated from the herd to prevent the spread of disease.
大型农场主可利用无线物联网应用来监控牛群的位置、状态与健康状况。通过这些信息，他们可以识别出生病的动物，从而将其与牛群分开，以防止疾病的传播。

2. Automation in Smart Greenhouses智能温室中的自动化

Traditional greenhouses control the environmental parameters through manual intervention or a proportional control mechanism, which often results in production loss, energy loss, and increased labor cost. 传统温室通过人工干预或比例控制机制来调节环境参数，这往往会导致生产损失、能源损失以及劳动力成本的增加。
IoT-driven smart greenhouses can intelligently monitor as well as control the climate, eliminating the need for manual intervention. Various sensors are deployed to measure the environmental parameters according to the specific requirements of the crop. That data is stored on a cloud-based platform for further processing and control with minimal manual intervention.物联网驱动的智能温室能够智能地监测和控制气候，无需人工干预。根据作物的特定需求，部署了各种传感器来测量环境参数。这些数据存储在基于云的平台中，以便在最少人工干预的情况下进行进一步处理和控制。
Agriculture is one of the major verticals to incorporate both ground-based and aerial drones for crop health assessment, irrigation, crop monitoring, crop spraying, planting, soil and field analysis, and other spheres/field/sector.农业是主要应用领域之一，该领域将地面无人机和空中无人机相结合，用于作物健康评估、灌溉、作物监测、作物喷洒、种植、土壤和田间分析以及其他领域。
Since drones collect multispectral, thermal, and visual imagery while flying, the data they gather provide farmers with insights into a whole array of metrics: plant health indices, plant counting and yield prediction, plant height measurement, canopy cover mapping, field water pond mapping, scouting reports, stockpile measuring, chlorophyll measurement, nitrogen content in wheat, drainage mapping, weed pressure mapping, and so on.由于无人机在飞行过程中会收集多光谱、热像和视觉图像，它们所收集的数据为农民提供了对一系列指标的详细信息：植物健康指数、植株计数和产量预测、植物高度测量、冠层覆盖度测绘、田间积水测绘、侦察报告、库存测量、叶绿素测量、小麦中的氮含量、排水测绘/水系测绘、杂草压力测绘等等。
Importantly, IoT-based smart farming doesn’t only target large-scale farming operations; it can add value to emerging trends in agriculture like organic farming, family farming, including breeding particular cattle and/or growing specific cultures,(the raising of plants or animals) preservation of particular or high-quality varieties, and enhance highly transparent farming to consumers, society and market consciousness.重要的是，基于物联网的智能农业不仅针对大规模农业经营；它还能为农业中的新兴趋势增添价值，如有机农业、家庭农业，包括饲养特定牛种和/或种植特定作物、保存特定或优质品种，以及增强对消费者、社会和市场的高度透明农业意识。
What’s Next in Smart Farming智慧农业的下一步是什么 Of course, all of these innovations are useless if they are not providing solutions to global problems. Here are two ways that smart farming is going to impact （influencing strongly） the future for the better.当然，如果这些创新不能为全球问题提供解决方案，那么它们就毫无用处。以下是智能农业将使未来变得更好的两种方式。

fluencing strongly） the future for the better.当然，如果这些创新不能为全球问题提供解决方案，那么它们就毫无用处。以下是智能农业将使未来变得更好的两种方式。

1. Internet of Food, or Farm 2020 食品互联网，或2020年农场

If we have the Internet of Things (IoT) and the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), why not have one for food? The European Commission project Internet of Food and Farm 2020 (IoF 2020), a part of Horizon 2020 Industrial Leadership, explores through research and regular conferences the potential of IoT technologies for the European food and farming industry.如果我们有物联网（IoT）和医疗物联网（IoMT），为何不建立一个食品物联网呢？欧盟委员会的“食品与农业物联网2020”（IoF 2020）项目是“地平线2020工业领导力”计划的一部分，该项目通过研究和定期会议，探索物联网技术在欧洲食品和农业领域的潜力。
IoT has fostered the belief that a smart network of sensors, actuators, cameras, robots, drones, and other connected devices will bring an unprecedented level of control and automated decision-making to agriculture, making possible an enduring ecosystem of innovation in this eldest of industries.物联网（IoT）催生了一种信念，即由传感器、执行器、摄像头、机器人、无人机和其他互联设备构成的智能网络将为农业带来前所未有的控制水平和自动化决策能力，使这一历史悠久的行业得以构建一个持久的创新生态系统。

2. Third Green Revolution第三次绿色革命

人机和其他互联设备构成的智能网络将为农业带来前所未有的控制水平和自动化决策能力，使这一历史悠久的行业得以构建一个持久的创新生态系统。

2. Third Green Revolution第三次绿色革命

Smart farming and IoT-driven agriculture are paving the way for what can be called a Third Green Revolution.智能农业和物联网驱动的农业正在为所谓的第三次绿色革命铺平道路。
Following the plant breeding and genetics revolutions, the Third Green Revolution is taking over agriculture. That revolution draws upon the combined application of data-driven analytics technologies, such as precision farming equipment, IoT, big data analytics, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), robotics, etc.继植物育种和遗传学革命之后，第三次绿色革命正在席卷农业领域。这场革命依赖于数据驱动分析技术的综合应用，如精准农业设备、物联网、大数据分析、无人机（UAVs）、机器人技术等。
In the future, as this smart farming revolution depicts, pesticide and fertilizer use will drop while overall efficiency will rise. IoT technologies will enable better food traceability, which in turn will lead to increased food safety. It will also be beneficial for the environment, through, for example, more efficient use of water, or optimization of treatments and inputs.未来，正如这场智能农业革命所描绘的，农药和化肥的使用量将下降，而整体效率将提高。物联网技术将使食品的可追溯性得到提升，从而增强食品安全。此外，通过更高效地利用水资源或优化处理和投入，还将对环境产生益处。
Therefore, smart farming has a real potential to deliver a more productive and sustainable form of agricultural production, based on a more precise and resource-efficient approach. New farms will finally realize the eternal dream of mankind. It’ll feed our population, which may explode to 9.6 billion by 2050. 因此，智能农业凭借其更精确且资源高效的方法，具有实现更高产、更可持续农业生产的真正潜力。新型农场将最终实现人类的永恒梦想。它将养活我们的人口，到2050年，全球人口可能会激增至96亿。

content:
Unit one  E-C Translation

1. “Four Modernizations” is intended to strengthen the industrial, agricultural, defense, and science and technology sectors as a means to rejuvenate the country. “四个现代化”旨在促进工业、农业、国防和科技领域发展，作为振兴国家的手段。
2. China is home to more than 1.4 billion people, with the population of some of its provinces being larger than that of some Western countries. 中国有14亿多人口，一些省份的人口比一些西方国家的人口还多。
3. By eradicating absolute poverty, China has taken a big step toward realizing common prosperity because rising income means increasing consumption, which in turn will promote development.

通过消除绝对贫困，中国向实现共同富裕迈出了一大步，因为收入的增加意味着消费的增加，而消费的增加又会促进发展。

4. Chinese modernization attaches great importance to developing harmony between material civilization and spiritual civilization, leading to the all-round development of human beings.

中国式现代化强调物质文明和精神文明的和谐发展，促进人类全面发展。

5. The new path to modernization with Chinese characteristics has transformed the fate of Chinese nation and exerted a profound influence on the course of world history.

具有中国特色的现代化道路改变了中华民族的命运，对世界历史的进程产生了深远的影响。
现代化是传统向现代的一种转变，涉及经济、社会、政治、文明等各个方面。中国式现代化拓展了发展中国家走向现代化的途径，是人类文明对现代化道路的新探索。党的二十大报告中 11 次提到中国式现代化，并赋予其明确定义和显著特征，指出教育、科技、人才是全面建设社会主义现代化国家的基础性、战略性支撑。在中国式现代化新道路上，坚持教育优先发展、科技自立自强、人才引领驱动，必将为全面推进中华民族伟大复兴提供强大动力。“四新”教育将为中国式现代化建设提供重要的人才和智力支持。
Modernization is a transformation from tradition to modernity, involving various aspects such as economy, society, politics, and culture, etc. Chinese path to modernization, a new exploration of human civilization on the road of modernization, has expanded the way for developing countries to move toward modernization. The report to the 20th National Congress of the Communist Party of China mentioned Chinese path to modernization 11 times, and gave it a clear definition and significant characteristics, pointing out that education, science and technology, and talents are the basic and strategic support for building a socialist modern country in all respects. On Chinese path to modernization, giving priority to education development, self-reliance in science and technology, and talent leadership will certainly provide a strong driving force for comprehensively promoting the great rejuvenation of the Chinese nation. The “Four New” education will provide important talents and intellectual support for Chinese path to modernization.
U2

content:
cation will provide important talents and intellectual support for Chinese path to modernization.
U2

1. As a large AI-powered language model, ChatGPT features strong semantic understanding and logical capabilities based on context.

ChatGPT作为一种大型人工智能语言模型，具有强大的语义理解能力和基于上下文的逻辑能力。

2. The use of ChatGPT-style conversational chatbots also raises concerns about ethics, copyright protection, academic cheating and leaks infringing on personal privacy.

使用ChatGPT风格的对话式聊天机器人也引发了人们对伦理道德、版权保护、学术作弊和侵犯个人隐私的泄密的担忧。

3. By incorporating AI in the work done using the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical radio Telescope, Chinese astronomers are trying to discover celestial objects and phenomena like pulsars.

通过将人工智能纳入500米口径球面射电望远镜的工作，中国天文学家正试图发现像脉冲星这样的天体和现象。

4. Chinese enterprises should step up efforts to roll out indigenous versions of the AI-powered chatbot, and increase investments to improve related algorithms and computing power.

中国企业应加大力度推出国产人工智能聊天机器人，并加大投资，提高相关算法和计算能力。

5. China filed more than half the world’s AI patent applications in 2021, and continued to lead the world in the number of AI journals, conference papers and relatedpublications.

2021年，中国提交的人工智能专利申请占全球一半以上，在人工智能期刊、会议论文和相关出版物数量上继续领先全球。
ChatGPT是美国OpenAI公司研发的聊天机器人程序，于2022年11月30日发布。它是人工智能技术驱动的自然语言处理工具，通过学习和理解人类的语言进行对话，根据聊天的上下文进行互动，能像人类一样聊天交流，甚至能完成撰写邮件、视频脚本、文案、代码以及翻译等基础任务。ChatGPT以GPT3.5为核心数据集，辅以进一步的RLHF技术训练，且加入更多人工监督进行微调。相比此前的GPT3，ChatGPT的主要提升点在于记忆能力，其可实现连续对话，极大地提升了对话交互模式下的用户体验。
ChatGPT, a chatbot program developed by OpenAI in the United States, was released on November 30, 2022. It is a natural language processing tool driven by artificial intelligence technology. It can communicate by learning and understanding human language, interact according to the context of the chat, chat and communicate like a human, and even complete basic tasks such as composing e-mails, video scripts, copywriting, code and translation. ChatGPT is built from a GPT3.5 core data set, with further training in RLHF techniques and additional human supervision for fine-tuning. Compared with previous GPT3, ChatGPT’s main improvement is its memory ability, which enables continuous conversations and greatly improves the user experience in conversational interaction mode.
U5

1. Since the term Industry 4.0 was coined in 2011, its associated technologies have developed to allow a data-rich, interconnected, and highly automated form of production called smart manufacturing.

自2011年“工业4.0”一词问世以来，其相关技术已经发展成为一种数据丰富、互联互通、高度自动化的生产形式，这就是智能制造。

2. Smart manufacturing helps manufacturers become more efficient, stay ahead of the competitive curve, and explore new business models and practices.

智能制造有助于制造商提高效率，在竞争曲线上保持领先地位，并有助于他们探索新的商业模式和实践。

3. For businesses interested in smart manufacturing but intimidated by financial or technical barriers, the good news is that they do not have to transition factories in one big leap.

对于那些对智能制造感兴趣但又被资金问题或技术壁垒吓倒的企业来说，好消息是他们不必在工厂转型方面一蹴而就。

4. Smart manufacturing can help a business become much more efficient with its resources, enhance worker safety, and facilitate worker training.

智能制造可以帮助企业提高资源利用效率，提高工人安全性，并为工人培训提供便利。

5. Simulation software can drastically reduce the amount of physical waste by moving many physical tests (such as vehicular crash tests) into simulated environments.

模拟软件可以通过将许多实物测试（如车辆碰撞测试）转移到模拟环境中，从而大幅减少实物的报废。

1. 经过几十年的奋斗，中国已经成为一个制造大国，但还不是制造强国。智能制造是推进制造强国战略的主攻方向。从现在到2035年，是中国制造业实现由大到强的关键时期。中国的智能制造发展总体可以分成数字化转型、智能化升级两个阶段。从现在到2028年深入推进“制造业数字化转型工程”，到2028年，数字化转型在全国得到大规模推广应用；从2028年到2035年深入推进“制造业智能化升级工程”，到2035年，新一代智能制造在全国制造业大规模推广应用，实现中国制造业的智能升级。

content:

After decades of development, China has become a big manufacturing country, but not a powerful one. Intelligent manufacturing is the main direction of promoting the strategy of becoming a manufacturing powerhouse. The period from now to 2035 is critical for China’s manufacturing industry to develop from being large to being powerful. The development of intelligent manufacturing in China can generally be divided into two stages: digital transformation and intelligent upgrading. From now to 2028, we will further promote the “Digital Transformation Project of Manufacturing Industry”, and by 2028, digital transformation will be widely promoted and applied throughout the country. From 2028 to 2035, we will further promote the “Intelligent Upgrading Project of Manufacturing Industry”. By 2035, the new generation of intelligent manufacturing will be widely promoted and applied in the national manufacturing industry, fulfilling the intelligent upgrading of China's manufacturing industry.
U7

1. Over the next decade, the idea of getting to work on time, heading out to the hinterlands for your family vacation or even going to the game will become much easier.

在接下来的10年里，准时上班，和家人一起去内陆地区度假或是去看比赛将变得容易得多。

2. “We will need to do something,” said Thilo Koslowski, the lead automotive analyst at research firm Gartner, who studies next-generation transportation, “given that we will continue to see more vehicles on the road but won’t be able to grow infrastructure at the same time.”

“我们还需要继续努力，”研究下一代交通工具的高德纳公司的首席汽车分析师蒂洛·科斯洛夫斯基说，“我们将继续看到更多的车辆上路，但却无法同时发展基础设施。”

3. A magnetic force lifts and propels the train using a minimal amount of energy compared to diesel-powered or electric-powered trains.

与柴油动力和电力火车相比，磁力提升和推动火车所需的能量最少。

4. That’s the idea behind autonomous driving, where you take your hands off the wheel and let the car do the driving for you.

这就是自动驾驶背后的理念：你无需把手放在方向盘上，汽车会为你驾驶。

5. We want to gain familiarity with future transport solutions in one area, to make it easier for industrial collaborators to come together and work together.

content:
ions in one area, to make it easier for industrial collaborators to come together and work together.

我们希望能更加熟悉某一领域未来交通的解决方案，能更好地与行业协作者们一起努力，共同合作。
骑着马和骆驼从一个地方到另一个地方，文明已经走过了漫长的道路。随着智能交通系统和物联网的出现，世界正进入下一个出行阶段——智能交通。智能交通和智能城市交通管理系统正在彻底改变城市的流通及应急响应方式，同时还能减少城市拥堵。如何实现？通过传感器、先进的通信技术、自动高速的网络共同实现。智慧交通不是未来的理论，世界上好几个城市正在实施这一方案。
Civilization has come a long way from riding horses and camels to get from place to place. With the emergence of intelligent transportation systems and the Internet of Things (IoT), the world is entering the next stage of movement—smart transportation. Smart transportation and smart city traffic management are revolutionizing how cities approach mobility and emergency response, while reducing congestion on city streets. How? With sensors, advanced communication technologies, automation and high-speed networks. Smart transportation is not just a theory for the future; it is being implemented today in several cities in the world.

U8

1. Agriculture is part of a vicious cycle in which farming leads to global warming, which in turn devastates agricultural production.

农业是耕作导致全球变暖的恶性循环的一部分，全球变暖反过来又破坏了农业生产。

2. New technologies will render many agricultural jobs obsolete as machines are able to accomplish the same tasks as humans.

新技术将使许多农业工作过时，因为机器能够完成与人类相同的任务。

3. Rapid population growth makes eradicating poverty, combating hunger and malnutrition, and increasing the coverage of health and education systems more difficult.

人口的快速增长使得消除贫困、消除饥饿和营养不良以及扩大卫生和教育系统的覆盖范围变得更加困难。

4. The global farming industry is facing many food safety challenges like non-standardized pest control, varying weather conditions, and also unpredictable contamination.

全球农业正面临许多食品安全挑战，如非标准化的虫害控制、多变的天气条件以及不可预测的污染。

5. With increasing demands and shortage of labor across the globe, agriculture automation and robots or commonly known as agribots are starting to gain attention among farmers.

content:
re automation and robots or commonly known as agribots are starting to gain attention among farmers.

随着全球劳动力需求的增加和短缺，农业自动化和机器人（通常被称为agribots）开始受到农民的关注。
智慧农业是智慧经济的重要内容，是依托物联网、云计算以及3S 技术等现代信息技术与农业生产相融合的产物，可以通过对农业生产环境的智能感知和数据分析，实现农业生产精准化管理和可视化诊断。智慧农业是智慧农业产业链，可以彻底升级传统的农业产业链，提高效率，改变产业结构。智慧农业是我国智慧经济的重要组成部分，并且处于我国现代农业发展的高级阶段。对于作为发展中国家的中国来说，加快智慧农业建设，利用物联网等现代信息技术提升农业生产管理水平，是实现本国农业转型升级的重要举措。
Smart agriculture is an important part of smart economy. It is the product of the integration of modern information technology such as the Internet of Things, cloud computing and 3S technology with agricultural production. It can realize the precision management and visual diagnosis of agricultural production through intelligent perception and data analysis of agricultural production environment. Smart agriculture is the industrial chain of smart agriculture, which can completely upgrade the traditional agricultural industry chain, improve efficiency and change the industrial structure. It is the important component of our country’s intelligent economy and is in the advanced stage of the development of mode

rn agriculture in our country. For China, as a developing country, accelerating the construction of smart agriculture and using modern information technologies such as the Internet of Things to improve agricultural production and management level are important measures to realize the transformation and upgrading of domestic agriculture.
课文翻译 Unit One Introductory Remarks Text A Understanding Chinese Path to Modernization The 20th National Congress of the Communist Party of China promoted Chinese modernization.党的二十大推动了中国式现代化进程。
Modernization is an important historical process that was initiated by the West since the Age of Discovery in the 15th century.现代化是一个重要的历史进程，自15世纪地理大发现以来，便由西方国家率先开启。
      The word “modernization” features a shift from the traditional agricultural society to a modern industrial one, rapidly developing science and technology and booming the economy with increasingly improved livelihoods. Modernization has become the common aspiration of people in all countries.“现代

化”一词体现了从传统农业社会向现代工业社会的转变，其特点是科学技术迅猛发展，经济蓬勃增长，人民生活水平持续提高。现代化已成为世界各国人民的共同愿望。

Modernization has become the common aspiration of people in all countries.“现代

化”一词体现了从传统农业社会向现代工业社会的转变，其特点是科学技术迅猛发展，经济蓬勃增长，人民生活水平持续提高。现代化已成为世界各国人民的共同愿望。

ple in all countries.“现代

化”一词体现了从传统农业社会向现代工业社会的转变，其特点是科学技术迅猛发展，经济蓬勃增长，人民生活水平持续提高。现代化已成为世界各国人民的共同愿望。

Chinese people have been familiar with the term since China adopted the concept of “Four Modernizations” in order to strengthen the industrial, agricultural, defense, and science and technology sectors as a means to rejuvenate the country.中国人民自中国提出“四个现代化”理念以来便熟悉这一概念，旨在通过加强工业、农业、国防和科技以振兴国家。
The highly-developed productivity and modern technology have paved the path to global modernization.高度发达的生产力和现代技术为全球现代化铺平了道路。
China’s h

istory, culture, demography and natural resources are different from those of the West, so China’s path to modernization differs from the West’s modernization despite both being based on highly developed productivity.
中国的历史、文化、人口和自然资源与西方不同，因此尽管两者都基于高度发达的生产力，但中国的现代化道路与西方的现代化存在差异。
Chinese modernization, as a product of the creative transformation and innovation-driven development of traditional Chinese culture, will suit the international community’s interests.中国式现代化作为中华优秀传统文化创造性转化和创新性发展的成果/产物，符合国际社会的共同利益。
Chinese modernization is socialist modernization under the leadership of the Communist Party of China.中国式现代化是中国共产党领导下的社会主义现代化。
It contains elements that are common to the modernization of Western countries, and also boasts features unique to the Chinese context.它包含了西方国家现代化的共同元素，也具有中国国情的独特特征。/它既包含了西方国家现代化进程中的共同元素，又具有符合中国国情的独特特征。

content:
e based on five factors, namely, the modernization of a huge population, pursuit of common prosperit

y, advancement of material, culture and ethics, promotion of harmony between humankind and nature, and realization of peaceful development.
中国式现代化的主要特征基于五个要素/方面：即：人口规模巨大的现代化、全体人民共同富裕的现代化、物质文明和精神文明相协调的现代化、人与自然和谐共生的现代化、走和平发展道路的现代化。
First, Chinese modernization is for a huge population.
首先，中国式现代化是人口规模巨大的现代化。
China is home to more than 1.4 billion people, with the population of some of its provinces being larger than that of some Western countries.中国拥有超过14亿人口，一些省份的人口规模已超过某些西方国家。So, China’s modernization is different from that of developed Western countries.因此，中国的现代化不同于西方发达国家的现代化。
The successful modernization of a country with a population that exceeds the combined total of all developed countries will completely change the international landscape. (structure/order).一个人口超过所有发达国家总和的国家成功实现现代化，将彻底改变国际格局。The development of a modern society of more than 1.4 billion people with shared prosperity will be a big contribution to overall global development, as China’s modernization will enlarge China’

s already huge market.一个拥有14亿人口的现代化社会的发展将重塑繁荣，对全球整体发展作出重大贡献，因为中国的现代化将进一步扩大其本已庞大的市场规模。
Second,Chinese modernization aims at common prosperity for everyone.Common prosperity is a	significant feature of Chinese modernization.其次，中国现代化致力于实现全体人民共同富裕。共同富裕是中国式现代化的重要特征。
By eradicating absolute poverty, China has taken a big step toward realizing common prosperity because rising income means increasing consumption, which in turn will promote development.通过消除绝对贫困，中国朝着实现共同富裕迈出了一大步，因为收入的增长意味着消费的增加，进而促进发展。Common prosperity will promote social fairness and justice. 共同富裕将促进社会公平正义。Efforts will be made to constantly enhance people’s sense of fulfillment, happiness and security.中国将不断努力增强人民的满足/成就感、幸福感和安全感。In contrast, Western-style modernization follows the logic of capital, emphasizes private ownership, capital allocation and the omnipotence of the market.相比之下，西方式现代化遵循资本逻辑，强调私有制、资本配置和市场万能。
Third,Chinese modernization balances material and cultural-ethical progress, enabling people to lead healthy and int

By eradicating absolute poverty, China has taken a big step toward realizing common prosperity because rising income means increasing consumption, which in turn will promote development.通过消除绝对贫困，中国朝着实现共同富裕迈出了一大步，因为收入的增长意味着消费的增加，进而促进发展。Common prosperity will promote social fairness and justice. 共同富裕将促进社会公平正义。Efforts will be made to constantly enhance people’s sense of fulfillment, happiness and security.中国将不断努力增强人民的满足/成就感、幸福感和安全感。In contrast, Western-style modernization follows the logic of capital, emphasizes private ownership, capital allocation and the omnipotence of the market.相比之下，西方式现代化遵循资本逻辑，强调私有制、资本配置和市场万能。
Third,Chinese modernization balances material and cultural-ethical progress, enabling people to lead healthy and int

content:
dernization balances material and cultural-ethical progress, enabling people to lead healthy and int

ellectually and culturally rich lives.
第3， 中国式现代化注重物质文明和精神文明相协调，使人民过上健康、充实的精神文化生活。Material, cultural and ethical advancement is a key feature of Chinese modernization.物质文明、精神文明与伦理道德的协同推进，是中国现代化建设的显著特征。/物质文明和精神文明的协调发展是中国式现代化的关键特征。In the modernization process of many countries, material advancement has been valued above cultural and ethical progress, leading to many social problems.在许多国家的现代化进程中，物质进步被置于文化和伦理进步之上，导致诸多社会问题。Philosophically speaking, one-sided development leads to alienation.从哲学上讲，片面的发展会导致异化。
Chinese modernization attaches great importance to/ places a high priority on developing harmony between material civilization and spiritual civilization, leading to the all-round development of human beings.中国式现代化高度重视物质文明和精神文明的协调发展，推动人的全面发展/促进人类全面发展。
Fourth, Chinese modernization features a harmonious coexistence between humanity and nature.第四，中国式现代化强调人与自然和谐共生。
That’s why China doesn’t believe in addressing environmental problems after the damage is done.因此，中国不主张“先污染后治理”。/Instead, China

takes steps in advance to prevent pollution and better protect the environment.而是采取预防措施防止污染，更好地保护环境。
It also creates more material and cultural wealth to meet people’s ever-increasing needs for a better life, and provides more eco-friendly goods to fulfill people’s demand for a clean, beautiful environment.同时，中国创造更多物质和精神财富以满足人民日益增长的美好生活需要，并提供更多生态产品以满足人民对优美生态环境的需求。
Chinese culture emphasizes the importance of unity between humans and nature, and regards humans as an integral part of nature, as is reflected in Taoist sage Laozi’s philosophy that “man is modeled on Earth, Earth on heaven, heaven on Tao, and Tao on nature”.中华文化强调“天人合一”，将人类视为自然不可分割的一部分，正如道家圣人老子所言：“人法地，地法天，天法道，道法自然”。
In traditional Chinese thought, human conduct should follow the harmonious rhythms of nature.在中国传统思想中，人的行为应该遵循大自然的和谐节奏。
And fifth, Chinese modernization follows the path of peaceful development.第五，中国式现代化走和平发展道路。
That’s why China has chosen its own path to modernization, so as to boost/promote its development an

content:
That’s why China has chosen its own path to modernization, so as to boost/promote its development an

d contribute to global modernization. 因此，中国选择了自身的现代化道路，以促进自身发展并为全球现代化作出贡献。/正因如此，中国选择了自己的现代化道路，以推动自身发展，并为全球现代化作出贡献。
It is also committed to/is dedicated to building a global community with a shared future and facilitating/fostering global peace and development.它还致力于构建人类命运共同体，并促进全球和平与发展。China became the world’s second-largest economy by realizing rapid economic growth,  participating in global economic and trade cooperation and adhering to the UN-based international order. 中国通过实现经济的快速增长成为了世界第二大经济体，它积极参与全球经济与贸易合作，遵守以联合国为核心的国际秩序。
  And it wants to realize modernization through innovation-driven/ innovation-led​development in order to improve the well-being/welfare of the Chinese people, instead of achieving prosperity/affluence at the expense of other countries.中国希望通过创新驱动实现现代化，以增进中国人民的福祉，而不是以牺牲别国利益为代价实现繁荣。/中国希望通过创新驱动发展实现现代化，以提升中国人民的福祉，而非以牺牲其他国家的利益为代价来换取自身的繁荣。
Chinese path to modernization follows/adheres to the regular laws governing modernization, steers the tide of human progress and cha

mpions the shared human values of peace, development, fairness, justice, democracy and freedom. 中国走向现代化的道路遵循现代化的一般规律，引领人类进步潮流，捍卫和平、发展、公平、正义、民主、自由的全人类共同价值观。
The new path to modernization with Chinese characteristics has transformed the fate of the Chinese nation and exerted a profound influence on the course of world history. 具有中国特色现代化道路改变了中华民族命运，对世界历史的进程产生了深远的影响。
It offers a new option for other countries to expedite their development while preserving their independence, and makes significant contributions to humankind’s search for different ways to achieve modernization.它为其他国家在保持自身独立性的同时加快发展提供了新的选择，并为人类探索实现现代化的不同途径做出了重大贡献。
The successful modernization of the world’s populous country will promote global modernization, and help accelerate human development worldwide. 世界人口大国的成功现代化将促进全球现代化，并有助于加速世界范围内的人类发展。
China, with 1.4 billion people, is the first most populous country in the world. 中国有14亿人口，是世界上人口第一大国。

content:
14亿人口，是世界上人口第一大国。
That’s why China, apart from following the regular laws governing global moderniza

tion, has chosen its own unique path to modernization. 这就是为什么中国除了遵循全球现代化的常规规律外，还选择了自己独特的现代化道路。
China’s new path has enriched the theory and practice of modernization, and the country has been contributing Chinese wisdom and Chinese solutions to the problems facing global modernization.中国的新道路丰富了现代化建设的理论与实践，中国一直在为全球现代化建设面临的问题贡献中国智慧和中国方案。
 Strong higher education is a key driving force, major contributor and important source of Chinese modernization. Therefore, higher education plays an irreplaceable strategic role in realizing modernization. 强大的高等教育是中国现代化的关键驱动力、主要贡献者和重要源泉。因此，高等教育在实现现代化中发挥着不可替代的战略作用。We need to accelerate educational reform, innovation and development, comprehensively improve the quality of independent talent training, focus on cultivating top-notch innovative talents, and support Chinese path to modernization with high-quality development. 我们需要加快教育改革、创新和发展，全面提高自主人才培养质量，着力培养一流创新人才，以高质量发展支撑中国走向现代化。The recently advocated “Four New” Education (i.e., new engineering, new liber

al arts, new medical science, and new agricultural science) construction is expected to provide strong talent support for Chinese path to modernization.近期倡导的“四新”教育（即新工科、新文科、新医科和新农科）建设，有望为中国走向现代化提供有力的人才支撑。
Unit Two Text A Is ChatGPT a High-Tech Breakthrough? ChatGPT是一项高科技突破吗？
Artificial intelligence-powered chatbot, ChatGPT, has been a hot topic of online discussions mainly for its “chatty” nature. 人工智能驱动的聊天机器人ChatGPT因其“健谈”的特性而成为网络热议的话题。Is ChatGPT a new technology revolution? In which areas has it made breakthroughs and what are its shortcomings? 是否是一场新的技术革命？它在哪些领域取得了突破？又存在哪些不足？Three experts share their views on these issues with China Daily’s Zhao Manfeng.ChatGPT三位专家就这些问题与《中国日报》的赵满峰分享了他们的看法。

 Strong higher education is a key driving force, major contributor and important source of Chinese modernization. Unit Two Text A Is ChatGPT a High-Tech Breakthrough? ChatGPT是一项高科技突破吗？
Artificial intelligence-powered chatbot, ChatGPT, has been a hot topic of online discussions mainly for its “chatty” nature. 人工智能驱动的聊天机器人ChatGPT因其“健谈”的特性而成为网络热议的话题。Is ChatGPT a new technology revolution? In which areas has it made breakthroughs and what are its shortcomings? 是否是一场新的技术革命？它在哪些领域取得了突破？又存在哪些不足？Three experts share their views on these issues with China Daily’s Zhao Manfeng.ChatGPT三位专家就这些问题与《中国日报》的赵满峰分享了他们的看法。
ChatGPT Is High-tech Progress, Not a RevolutionChatGPT是高科技进步，而非革命       Jing Jiwu, a professor at the University of the Chinese Academy of Sciences.中国科学院大学教授景继武。
      ChatGPT is a powerful chatbot developed by OpenAI and launched in November 2022. ChatGPT是由OpenAI开发并于2022年11月推出的强大聊天机器人。Built on top

content:
eveloped by OpenAI and launched in November 2022. ChatGPT是由OpenAI开发并于2022年11月推出的强大聊天机器人。Built on top

of OpenAI’s GPT-3 family of large language models, it engages in human-like dialogue when prompted. 它基于OpenAI的GPT-3大型语言模型系列构建，在收到提示时能够进行类似人类的对话。It is designed to respond in a natural, intuitive way and has numerous potential uses.它旨在以自然、直观的方式作出回应，并具有众多潜在用途。
From a scientific point of view, there is a need to closely watch the development of such technologies before reaching a conclusion about their efficacy and helpful nature. 从科学的角度来看，在就这些技术的有效性和益处得出结论之前，有必要密切关注其发展情况。Technology progresses step by step. 技术是循序渐进的。So we should not panic thinking that ChatGPT will take over jobs that till now need humans to fulfill.因此，我们不应恐慌地认为ChatGPT将取代目前仍需要人类来完成的工作。
      ChatGPT is an example of high-tech progress, not a revolution.ChatGPT是高科技进步的一个例子，而非一场革命。
First, ChatGPT has made quite some progress in natural language processing, yet it still has a long way to go to completely ape the human mind and language faculty, because when it comes to professional writing or execution of a job, ChatGPT at ti

mes writes plausible-sounding but incorrect or nonsensical answers. 首先，ChatGPT在自然语言处理方面已取得相当大的进展，但要完全模仿人类思维和语言能力，仍有很长的路要走，因为在专业写作或执行任务时，ChatGPT有时会写出听起来合理但不正确或荒谬的答案。It might give some good answers to most of the general questions, and it can produce articles, essays, jokes and even poetry with a little prompting, yet it is no substitute for professional advice, such as medical prognosis and prescriptions, or legal advice. 它或许能对大多数一般性问题给出一些好的答案，并且只需稍加提示就能生成文章、散文、笑话甚至诗歌，但它并不能替代专业建议，如医疗诊断和处方，或法律咨询。
      Second, ChatGPT is still a product with very limited application. It has the potential to be used for enhancing search engine functionality but, as a pre-trained language model, it will have limited use.其次，ChatGPT仍然是一个应用范围非常有限的产品。它有潜力用于增强搜索引擎功能，但作为一个预训练语言模型，其用途将十分有限。
      It is new technology, no doubt, which we should study, but investors should not blindly invest huge amounts to further develop ChatGPT, because it may not turn out to be successful and create bubbles in the high-tech mar

hrough feedback.ChatGPT的另一大特色是其通过反馈进行强化学习的能力。 It can recalculate figures or rewrite reports according to the user’s feedback on the answer. 它能根据用户对答案的反馈重新计算数据或重写报告。And this is one of the reasons why its performance is being optimized.这也是其性能不断优化的原因之一。
     Yet it still has its shortcomings. 然而，它仍存在不足。For one thing, it could only be applied in a limited number of fields as it is a content-generating model. 首先，由于它是一个内容生成模型，因此只能应用于有限数量的领域。And the content it generates depends on what it has been pre-trained in. 而且，它生成的内容取决于其预先训练的内容。Therefore, despite writing articles, essays, jokes and even poetry, ChatGPT could be used only as reference, just like a search engine.因此，尽管ChatGPT可以撰写文章、散文、笑话甚至诗歌，但它只能作为参考，就像搜索引擎一样。 It can never be a substitute for professional, in-depth knowledge ad research.它永远无法替代专业、深入的知识和研究。

content:
一些简单、重复性较强且创新性较低的工作，甚至一些白领工作，都可能被大型语言模型所取代。 But it is not infallible, and its potential risks have n

ot been thoroughly studied.但ChatGPT并非绝对可靠，其潜在风险尚未得到深入研究。 You can talk to it, acquire general knowledge or even get entertained by it, but it cannot be the sole source of your information, let alone take your place.你可以与它对话，获取一般知识，甚至从中获得娱乐，但它不能成为你信息的唯一来源，更不能取代你。
It is an AI program. And although its functions are very helpful to humans, they should not be deified. 它是一个人工智能程序。尽管其功能对人类非常有帮助，但我们不应将其神化。First, AI itself has been on the cusp of rising to greater heights in recent years. 首先，近年来，人工智能本身已处于更上一层楼的风口浪尖。What’s more, ChatGPT can meet some “lazy” people’s need of avoiding repetitive and boring mental work. 此外，ChatGPT能够满足一些“懒惰”之人避免重复且枯燥脑力劳动的需求。But mental work itself is part of the human cognitive faculty.但脑力劳动本身是人类认知能力的一部分。
   Search engines used to retrieve large volumes of information, enabling people to view page after page. 搜索引擎曾用于检索大量信息，使人们能够一页接一页地浏览。With the aid of ChatGPT, search engines can present more precise answers to complex questions, and users can now get information in a

more relaxing way of dialogue.借助ChatGPT，搜索引擎能够为复杂问题提供更精确的答案，用户现在可以通过更轻松的对话方式获取信息。
     China has also developed its own large-scale language models, such as Huawei’s Pangu NLP models, Baidu’s ERNIE, Tencent’s HUNYUAN NLP models, and Alibaba’s AliceMind, and the performance of the models in natural language processing has been excellent.中国也开发了自己的大型语言模型，如华为的盘古自然语言处理（NLP）模型、百度的ERNIE、腾讯的浑元自然语言处理模型以及阿里巴巴的AliceMind，这些模型在自然语言处理方面的表现非常出色。 But China’s large-scale language models need to focus more on deeper technologies, and should not blindly follow trends.但中国的大型语言模型需要更加关注更深层次的技术，不应盲目跟风。
     Since ChatGPT is also a dialogue model, China needs more custom models in specific fields, such as modern Chinese language, ancient Chinese language, minority languages, and dialects, to break language barriers among people.
     由于ChatGPT也是一个对话模型，中国需要在特定领域（如现代汉语、古汉语、少数民族语言和方言）开发更多定制模型，以打破人与人之间的语言障碍。

Unit Five Smart  Manufacturing智能制造 Text A Smart Manufacturing: The Future of Making Is Digital智能制造：制造业的未来是数字化 The technologies of the Fourth Industrial Revolution (Industry 4.0)—connected robotics, 3D printing, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things—made smart manufacturing possible and sped innovation to a dizzying clip.第四次工业革命（工业4.0）的技术——互联机器人、3D打印、云计算、人工智能和物联网——使智能制造成为可能，并极大地加速了创新进程，使其达到令人目眩的速度。
The dawn of mechanical manufacturing happened during the First Industrial Revolution at about the end of the 18th century, powered by water and steam. 机械制造业的开端发生在18世纪末的第一次工业革命期间，其动力源于水和蒸汽。Two centuries later, electricity enabled the Second Industrial Revolution’s mass-production assembly lines and division of labor. 两个世纪后，电力推动了第二次工业革命中大规模生产流水线和劳动分工的出现。Rapid progress led to the Third Industrial Revolution around 1970, when computers and information technology automated some industrial processes and led to more sophisticated communication.1970年左右，随着计算机和信息技术的快速发展，第

三次工业革命应运而生，这些技术使一些工业流程实现了自动化，并带来了更先进的通信方式。
Since the term Industry 4.0 was coined（create，invent） in 2011, its associated technologies have developed to allow a data-rich, interconnected, and highly automated form of production called smart manufacturing.自2011年“工业4.0”这一术语被提出以来（一词问世以来），与其相关技术不断发展，催生了一种数据丰富、互联互通且高度自动化的生产形式，即智能制造。
What Is Smart Manufacturing?什么是智能制造？
     Smart manufacturing refers to the widespread digitization of all manufacturing practices, from the factory floor to all aspects of business. It includes product design, supply chain, production, distribution, and sales.智能制造指的是从工厂车间到企业运营各个环节的所有生产流程实现全面数字化。涵盖产品设计、供应链、生产、分销以及销售。
Smart manufacturing leverages Industry 4.0, which is characterized by interconnected cyber-physical systems such as intelligent robots and machines that can self-diagnose and warn of possible failures.智能制造借助了工业4.0技术这一概念，其特点是具备互联的网联物理系统，如具备自我诊断功能和能够发出可能故障警报的智能机器人和机械设备。
The proliferating IoT brings more powerful devices and machines with smart sensors

Unit Five Smart  Manufacturing智能制造 Text A Smart Manufacturing: The Future of Making Is Digital智能制造：制造业的未来是数字化 The technologies of the Fourth Industrial Revolution (Industry 4.0)—connected robotics, 3D printing, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things—made smart manufacturing possible and sped innovation to a dizzying clip.第四次工业革命（工业4.0）的技术——互联机器人、3D打印、云计算、人工智能和物联网——使智能制造成为可能，并极大地加速了创新进程，使其达到令人目眩的速度。
The dawn of mechanical manufacturing happened during the First Industrial Revolution at about the end of the 18th century, powered by water and steam. 机械制造业的开端发生在18世纪末的第一次工业革命期间，其动力源于水和蒸汽。Two centuries later, electricity enabled the Second Industrial Revolution’s mass-production assembly lines and division of labor. 两个世纪后，电力推动了第二次工业革命中大规模生产流水线和劳动分工的出现。Rapid progress led to the Third Industrial Revolution around 1970, when computers and information technology automated some industrial processes and led to more sophisticated communication.1970年左右，随着计算机和信息技术的快速发展，第

三次工业革命应运而生，这些技术使一些工业流程实现了自动化，并带来了更先进的通信方式。
Since the term Industry 4.0 was coined（create，invent） in 2011, its associated technologies have developed to allow a data-rich, interconnected, and highly automated form of production called smart manufacturing.自2011年“工业4.0”这一术语被提出以来（一词问世以来），与其相关技术不断发展，催生了一种数据丰富、互联互通且高度自动化的生产形式，即智能制造。
What Is Smart Manufacturing?什么是智能制造？
     Smart manufacturing refers to the widespread digitization of all manufacturing practices, from the factory floor to all aspects of business. It includes product design, supply chain, production, distribution, and sales.智能制造指的是从工厂车间到企业运营各个环节的所有生产流程实现全面数字化。涵盖产品设计、供应链、生产、分销以及销售。
Smart manufacturing leverages Industry 4.0, which is characterized by interconnected cyber-physical systems such as intelligent robots and machines that can self-diagnose and warn of possible failures.智能制造借助了工业4.0技术这一概念，其特点是具备互联的网联物理系统，如具备自我诊断功能和能够发出可能故障警报的智能机器人和机械设备。
The proliferating IoT brings more powerful devices and machines with smart sensors

content:
能故障警报的智能机器人和机械设备。
The proliferating IoT brings more powerful devices and machines with smart sensors

that upload continuous streams of usage data to the cloud for analysis. 物联网的迅速发展带来了更多功能强大的设备和机器，这些设备和机器配备了智能传感器，能够持续将使用数据流上传至云端进行分析。
Those Big Data sets are crunched using AI with machine learning that becomes more accurate and predictive as it absorbs more data.这些大数据集通过人工智能和机器学习进行处理，随着吸收更多数据，其准确性和预测性不断提高。
Automation and data connectivity create more agile supply chains, in which ships and trucks “talk” to warehouses, autonomous or semi-autonomous vehicles, and drones. 自动化和数据连接创造了更敏捷的供应链，其中船舶和卡车能够与仓库、自动驾驶或半自动驾驶车辆以及无人机进行“对话”。Mobile robots and cobots (collaborative robots) are also folded into this process, making shipping and logistics more automated.移动机器人和协作机器人（cobots）也被纳入这一流程，使航运和物流更加自动化。
Smart manufacturing helps manufacturers become more efficient, stay ahead of the competitive curve, and explore new business models and practices.智能制造助力制造商提升效率，保持竞争优势，并探索新的商业模式和实践方法。Benefits of Adopting Smart Manufacturing 采用智能制造的好处     Businesses can adopt smart manufacturing to help streamline proc

esses, increase productivity, stay competitive, and prepare for the future—including for unprecedented events, such as a pandemic.企业可以采用智能制造来帮助简化流程、提高生产力、保持竞争力，并为未来做好准备——包括应对前所未有的事件，如大流行病。
An ongoing poll from the nonprofit Manufacturing Enterprise Solutions Association (MESA) asks companies what is the main challenge that is slowing their progress toward smart manufacturing.非营利组织制造企业解决方案协会（MESA）正在进行的一项民意调查询问企业，阻碍其向智能制造转型的主要挑战是什么。
About 58% cited financial concerns or lack of knowledge about the related technologies. To be sure, adopting smart manufacturing requires significant investments of financial and human resources and involves some risk.约58%的企业表示，主要原因是财务方面的担忧或对相关技术缺乏了解。诚然，采用智能制造需要大量财力和人力资源的投资，并存在一定的风险。
However, the 2019 “Smart Factory Study” from Deloitte and the Manufacturers Alliance concludes that any manufacturer, whether it has begun smart-factory initiatives or not, can derive value for its business by doing so—and that the value of those initiatives generally outweighs t

esses, increase productivity, stay competitive, and prepare for the future—including for unprecedented events, such as a pandemic.企业可以采用智能制造来帮助简化流程、提高生产力、保持竞争力，并为未来做好准备——包括应对前所未有的事件，如大流行病。
An ongoing poll from the nonprofit Manufacturing Enterprise Solutions Association (MESA) asks companies what is the main challenge that is slowing their progress toward smart manufacturing.非营利组织制造企业解决方案协会（MESA）正在进行的一项民意调查询问企业，阻碍其向智能制造转型的主要挑战是什么。
About 58% cited financial concerns or lack of knowledge about the related technologies. To be sure, adopting smart manufacturing requires significant investments of financial and human resources and involves some risk.约58%的企业表示，主要原因是财务方面的担忧或对相关技术缺乏了解。诚然，采用智能制造需要大量财力和人力资源的投资，并存在一定的风险。
However, the 2019 “Smart Factory Study” from Deloitte and the Manufacturers Alliance concludes that any manufacturer, whether it has begun smart-factory initiatives or not, can derive value for its business by doing so—and that the value of those initiatives generally outweighs t

content:
ive value for its business by doing so—and that the value of those initiatives generally outweighs t

he financial and operational risks.然而，德勤和制造商联盟2019年的“智能工厂研究”得出结论，任何制造商，无论是否已开始实施智能工厂计划，都可以通过这样做为其业务带来价值，而这些计划的价值通常大于财务和运营风险。
The study broke respondents into two groups: Group A (49% of companies) that had no ongoing smart-factory initiatives, and Group B (51%) that had some level of ongoing smart-factory initiatives. 该研究将受访者分为两组：A组（占公司总数的49%）没有正在进行的智能工厂计划，B组（占51%）有一定程度的正在进行的智能工厂计划。The study determined that Group A’s productivity index for 2015–2018 likely declined 2.3% per year while Group B saw average annual productivity rise 3.3% during the same period.该研究确定，2015年至2018年期间，A组的生产率指数可能每年下降2.3%，而同期B组的平均年生产率则上升了3.3%。Furthermore, the Group B companies experienced a 10% average increase in production output and an 11% average increase in factory capacity utilization.此外，B组公司的生产产量平均增长了10%，工厂产能利用率平均增长了11%。
Most of the companies not yet implementing smart manufacturing still saw its importance. 大多数尚未实施智能制造的公司仍然认识到了其重要性。In the study, 86% of manufacturers surveyed believe that within five years

content:
大多数尚未实施智能制造的公司仍然认识到了其重要性。In the study, 86% of manufacturers surveyed believe that within five years

, smart-manufacturing solutions will be the main driver of competitiveness, which highlights how important smart-manufacturing capabilities will be to enable manufacturers to be competitive in the coming years. 在这项研究中，86%的受访制造商认为，在未来五年内，智能制造解决方案将成为竞争力的主要驱动力，这凸显了智能制造能力对于制造商在未来几年保持竞争力的重要性。It is only a matter of time before all manufacturers will have to adopt smart-manufacturing technologies.所有制造商都将不得不采用智能制造技术，这只是一个时间问题。
How to Start the Smart-Manufacturing Journey?如何开启智能制造之旅？
    For businesses interested in smart manufacturing but intimidated by financial or technical barriers, the good news is that they do not have to transition factories in one big leap.对于那些对智能制造感兴趣但因财务或技术障碍而望而却步的企业而言，好消息是它们不必一次性对工厂进行大规模改造。/好消息是它们无需一步到位地完成工厂转型。In fact, the “Smart Factory Study” shows that small steps often lead to big wins.事实上，“智能工厂研究”表明，小步快走往往能带来大收获。 Successful companies from the study usually started by securing support from executives in the C-suite—especially the chief technology officer—and laun

, smart-manufacturing solutions will be the main driver of competitiveness, which highlights how important smart-manufacturing capabilities will be to enable manufacturers to be competitive in the coming years. 在这项研究中，86%的受访制造商认为，在未来五年内，智能制造解决方案将成为竞争力的主要驱动力，这凸显了智能制造能力对于制造商在未来几年保持竞争力的重要性。It is only a matter of time before all manufacturers will have to adopt smart-manufacturing technologies.所有制造商都将不得不采用智能制造技术，这只是一个时间问题。
How to Start the Smart-Manufacturing Journey?如何开启智能制造之旅？
    For businesses interested in smart manufacturing but intimidated by financial or technical barriers, the good news is that they do not have to transition factories in one big leap.对于那些对智能制造感兴趣但因财务或技术障碍而望而却步的企业而言，好消息是它们不必一次性对工厂进行大规模改造。/好消息是它们无需一步到位地完成工厂转型。In fact, the “Smart Factory Study” shows that small steps often lead to big wins.事实上，“智能工厂研究”表明，小步快走往往能带来大收获。 Successful companies from the study usually started by securing support from executives in the C-suite—especially the chief technology officer—and laun

content:
by securing support from executives in the C-suite—especially the chief technology officer—and laun

ching multiple small projects with low initial investments. 研究中那些成功的公司通常首先会获得高管（尤其是首席技术官）的支持，并启动多个初期投资较低的小项目。By tying those projects to measurable business metrics, they could use any early successes as an incentive to seek additional incremental investments.通过将这些项目与可衡量的业务指标联系起来，它们可以利用任何早期成功作为激励，寻求更多的增量投资。
It is suggested that the manufacturers starting out on a smart-manufacturing journey should begin by making their analog data digital: Eliminate （to remove or get rid of sth/sb排除；清除；消除） manual analog processes, and find a system that captures everything in one digital database. 建议那些刚刚踏上智能制造之旅的制造商，首先应将他们的模拟数据数字化：消除手动模拟流程，并找到一个能将所有内容捕获到一个数字数据库中的系统。
That’s the foundational data on which to build feedback loops by inputting more information from factory-floor processes. 这是通过输入更多来自工厂车间流程的信息来构建反馈回路的基础数据。This additional input could come from sensor data, but it could also be from workers inputting data at different points in the process.这些额外的输入可能来自传感器数据，但也可能来自工人在流程中不同环节输入的数据。
Once manufactu

rers’ problems are revealed by data, they can start thinking about making changes on multiple dimensions—that’s where introducing more computation and algorithms can help. 一旦制造商的问题通过数据被揭示出来，他们就可以开始考虑在多个维度上进行变革——这就是引入更多计算和算法能提供帮助的地方。
“You let the algorithms give you different exploration possibilities so you can figure out the best path forward,” Jonnalagadda, Autodesk vice president of go-to-market strategy and marketing for Design and Manufacturing, says. “你让算法为你提供不同的探索可能性，这样你就能找出最佳的前进道路，”欧特克公司负责设计与制造领域市场战略与营销的副总裁乔纳拉加达说。“Now, you have a digital backbone. You have input and feedback loops giving you insights and the help of algorithms that give you options for continuing to refine and optimize factory-floor operations.”“现在，你拥有了一个数字中枢。输入和反馈循环为你提供洞察力，而算法则帮助你继续完善和优化工厂车间的运营。”

You have input and feedback loops giving you insights and the help of algorithms that give you options for continuing to refine and optimize factory-floor operations.”“现在，你拥有了一个数字中枢。输入和反馈循环为你提供洞察力，而算法则帮助你继续完善和优化工厂车间的运营。”
   Impact of Smart Manufacturing 智能制造的影响    Smart manufacturing can help a business become much more efficient with its resources, enhance worker safety, and facilitate worker training.  智能制造可以帮助企业大幅提高资源利用效率，增强工人安全，并促

content:
ts resources, enhance worker safety, and facilitate worker training.  智能制造可以帮助企业大幅提高资源利用效率，增强工人安全，并促

进工人培训。It can also make a business more agile, according to MESA Knowledge Committee Chair Khris Kammer. 据制造企业解决方案协会（Manufacturing Enterprise Solutions Association，简称MESA）知识委员会主席克里斯·卡默（Khris Kammer）表示，智能制造还能使企业更加灵活。
“Look at the number of companies during the pandemic that pivoted to manufacturing other things, whether personal protective equipment (PPE) or related medical devices,” Kammer says, “they could do that only because they had the agility to introduce a new product into their manufacturing environment very quickly and retool, retrain, and execute in a very rapid fashion./way”“看看疫情期间有多少公司转而生产其他产品，无论是个人防护装备（PPE）还是相关医疗设备，”卡默说，“他们之所以能做到这一点，只是因为他们具备迅速将新产品引入制造环境、快速重组、快速再培训和快速执行的灵活性。”
Smart-manufacturing firms can be agile with their products and participate in the “mass customization (MC)” trend that lets manufacturers deliver low-volume, high-variability products. 智能制造企业能够灵活调整其产品，并顺应“大规模定制（MC）”的趋势，该趋势使制造商能够提供小批量、高可变性的产品。Smart manufacturing has the potential to improve outcomes for

individual businesses and the manufacturing industry as a whole.智能制造有潜力改善单个企业和整个制造业的业绩。
   Another new business model that smart manufacturing has enabled, manufacturing as a service (MaaS), could help make the supply chain more resilient to disruptions like those in 2020. 智能制造所催生的另一种新型商业模式——制造即服务（MaaS），可能有助于使供应链更能抵御像2020年那样的中断。
Just as ride-share services rely on machine learning to match drivers with passengers very quickly, MaaS requires machine learning to quickly and effectively match manufacturers with customers.正如共享出行服务/网约车服务依靠机器学习来快速匹配司机和乘客一样，MaaS也需要机器学习来快速有效地匹配制造商和客户。
   The “Accelerating Smart Manufacturing” report focuses on another development with industry-wide implications: the “network effect” benefits of smart-manufacturing ecosystems, wherein many companies and entities meaningfully work together using advanced, connected technologies to meet/fulfil common objectives.《加速智能制造》报告聚焦于另一项具有行业广泛影响的发展：智能制造生态系统的“网络效应”优势，即众多公司和实体利用先进、互联的技术进行有意义的合作，以实现共同目标。
