ts resources, enhance worker safety, and facilitate worker training.  智能制造可以帮助企业大幅提高资源利用效率，增强工人安全，并促

进工人培训。It can also make a business more agile, according to MESA Knowledge Committee Chair Khris Kammer. 据制造企业解决方案协会（Manufacturing Enterprise Solutions Association，简称MESA）知识委员会主席克里斯·卡默（Khris Kammer）表示，智能制造还能使企业更加灵活。
“Look at the number of companies during the pandemic that pivoted to manufacturing other things, whether personal protective equipment (PPE) or related medical devices,” Kammer says, “they could do that only because they had the agility to introduce a new product into their manufacturing environment very quickly and retool, retrain, and execute in a very rapid fashion./way”“看看疫情期间有多少公司转而生产其他产品，无论是个人防护装备（PPE）还是相关医疗设备，”卡默说，“他们之所以能做到这一点，只是因为他们具备迅速将新产品引入制造环境、快速重组、快速再培训和快速执行的灵活性。”
Smart-manufacturing firms can be agile with their products and participate in the “mass customization (MC)” trend that lets manufacturers deliver low-volume, high-variability products. 智能制造企业能够灵活调整其产品，并顺应“大规模定制（MC）”的趋势，该趋势使制造商能够提供小批量、高可变性的产品。Smart manufacturing has the potential to improve outcomes for

individual businesses and the manufacturing industry as a whole.智能制造有潜力改善单个企业和整个制造业的业绩。
   Another new business model that smart manufacturing has enabled, manufacturing as a service (MaaS), could help make the supply chain more resilient to disruptions like those in 2020. 智能制造所催生的另一种新型商业模式——制造即服务（MaaS），可能有助于使供应链更能抵御像2020年那样的中断。
Just as ride-share services rely on machine learning to match drivers with passengers very quickly, MaaS requires machine learning to quickly and effectively match manufacturers with customers.正如共享出行服务/网约车服务依靠机器学习来快速匹配司机和乘客一样，MaaS也需要机器学习来快速有效地匹配制造商和客户。
   The “Accelerating Smart Manufacturing” report focuses on another development with industry-wide implications: the “network effect” benefits of smart-manufacturing ecosystems, wherein many companies and entities meaningfully work together using advanced, connected technologies to meet/fulfil common objectives.《加速智能制造》报告聚焦于另一项具有行业广泛影响的发展：智能制造生态系统的“网络效应”优势，即众多公司和实体利用先进、互联的技术进行有意义的合作，以实现共同目标。
   Smart-manufacturin

content:
智能制造》报告聚焦于另一项具有行业广泛影响的发展：智能制造生态系统的“网络效应”优势，即众多公司和实体利用先进、互联的技术进行有意义的合作，以实现共同目标。
   Smart-manufacturin

g ecosystems are mutually beneficial in any circumstance because they share access to collective wisdom and innovation.智能制造生态系统在任何情况下都是互惠互利的，因为它们能够共享集体智慧和创新资源。The report found that Fortune 500 manufacturers with more than 15 strategic alliances doubled year-over-year revenue growth in 2019 compared to those with fewer alliances. 报告发现，与战略联盟较少的《财富》500强制造商相比，拥有超过15个战略联盟的制造商在2019年的收入同比增长翻了一番。
Companies within smart-manufacturing ecosystems also experienced a faster pace of introducing products and services, expanded innovation capacity, faster maturity of their digital technology, and greater efficiencies that reduced operational costs.智能制造生态系统内的公司还加快了产品和服务的推出速度，扩大了创新能力，数字技术成熟度更快，效率更高，从而降低了运营成本。
    Smart Manufacturing’s Impact on Sustainability智能制造对可持续性的影响 Some smart-manufacturing technologies inherently promote efficient resource use. 一些智能制造技术本身就促进了资源的有效利用。Simulation software, for instance, can drastically reduce the amount of physical waste by moving many physical tests (such as vehicul

ar crash tests) into simulated environments.例如，仿真软件可以通过将许多物理测试（如车辆碰撞测试）转移到模拟环境中，大幅减少物理浪费。 Simulation can also predict the durability and lifespan of different materials and test alternate materials for end products with the lowest impact on the planet. 仿真还可以预测不同材料的耐用性和使用寿命，并测试替代材料，以生产对地球影响最小的最终产品。
Design for Manufacturing (DFM) can also introduce sustainability benefits into a product. For example, generative design software uses AI to generate an abundance of design options that can reduce product weight and the amount of material required while maintaining strength and cost.
   面向制造的设计（DFM）也可以为产品带来可持续性优势。例如，生成设计软件利用人工智能生成大量设计选项，这些选项可以在保持强度和成本的同时，减少产品重量和所需材料量。
With the right intention, smart manufacturing can actively support sustainability goals. 本着正确的初衷，智能制造能够积极助力实现可持续发展目标。
The Smart Factory@Wichita (a joint effort from Wichita State University and Deloitte) will include a full-scale production line demonstrating how to join existing manufacturing assets with smart-manufacturing tec

hnologies such as robotics systems, 3D printers, 3D laser scanners, AR/VR assets, simulation and visualization software, and more. 威奇托智能工厂（由威奇托州立大学和德勤共同打造）将包含一条全面生产线，展示如何将现有制造资产与智能制造技术相结合，如机器人系统、3D打印机、3D激光扫描仪、增强现实/虚拟现实（AR/VR）资产、模拟和可视化软件等。The brand-new facility is also a net-zero smart building on a smart grid, and the program aims to demonstrate how a smart-factory’s advancing technology can address sustainability by streamlining operations to reduce environmental impact.这座全新的设施还是一座位于智能电网上的零能耗智能建筑，该项目旨在展示智能工厂的先进技术如何通过优化运营来减少对环境的影响，从而促进可持续发展。

Unit Seven  Smart Transportation Text A Five Future Transportation Technologies That Will Actually Happen未来将真正实现的五大交通技术 Over the next decade, the idea of getting to work on time, heading out to the hinterlands for your family vacation or even going to the game will become much easier.在未来十年，准时上班、前往偏远地区度假甚至去看比赛都将变得更加容易。Trains will use new magnetic rail systems. 火车将使用新型磁悬浮轨道系统。And an amazing new “hyperloop” train will speed along at 800 miles per hour.而令人惊叹的新型“超级高铁/超高速列车”将以每小时800英里的速度疾驰。
    The best part? These innovations are not just spinning their wheels. They are set to debut within the next 10 years or have already started transporting us.最棒的是？这些创新并非只是空谈。它们将在未来10年内首次亮相，或者已经开始为我们提供运输服务了。
“New technologies have the potential to make our roads and transit systems safer, greener and more efficient,” said Gregory Winfree, the administrator of the Department of Transportation’s Research and Innovative Technology Administration. “We are working hard to ensure that these technologies can be integrated safely into our existing system.”“新技术有潜力让我们的道路和交通系统变得更安全、更环保、更高效，”美国交通部研究与创新技术管理局局长格雷戈里·温弗里说。“我们正在努力确保这些技术能够安全地融入我们的现有系统。”
“We will need to do something,” said Thilo Koslowski, the lead automotive analyst at research firm Gartner, who studies next-generation transportation, “given that we will continue to see more vehicles on the road but won’t be able to grow infrastructure at the same time. We have to get smarter/wiser about using that infrastructure and/or innovate in passenger vehicles and mobility.”“鉴于我们将继续看到道路上出现更多车辆，但基础设施却无法同时增长，我们需要采取一些措施，”研究公司高德纳（Gartner）的首席汽车分析师蒂洛·科斯洛夫斯基（Thilo Koslowski）表示，“我们必须更明智地利用现有基础设施，或者在乘用车和出行方式上进行创新。”科斯洛夫斯基专门研究下一代交通工具。

1. Hyperloop1. 超级高铁/超高速列车

1. Hyperloop1. 超级高铁/超高速列车

One of the most exciting innovations in transportation has to be the hyperloop train. 交通领域最令人兴奋的创新之一非超级高铁莫属。Rising on nearly airless tubes at 800 mph, the train will transport you from LA to San Francisco in just 30 minutes.这种列车在近乎真空的管道中以800英里/小时的速度飞驰，仅需30分钟即可将你从洛杉矶送至旧金山。 Elon Musk announced a design scheme, but we have learned the concept is more than a pipe dream—it is now a real technology in development.埃隆·马斯克曾公布过其设计方案，但我们了解到，这一概念绝非空想——它现已成为一项正在开发中的真实技术。
“We’re moving toward conceptual design,” said Dr. Patricia Galloway, the co-leader of Hyperloop Transportation Technologies Inc., hinting at more than just a rough design sketch and an actual concept, something that is concrete and verifiable in the near future.“我们正在向概念设计迈进，”超级高铁运输技术股份有限公司（Hyperloop Transportation Technologies Inc.）的联合负责人帕特里夏·加洛韦（Patricia Galloway）博士说道，她暗示这不仅仅是一个粗略的设计草图和一个实际的概念，而是一个在不久的将来可以具体实现和验证的东西。
  “On paper, hyperloop is both cheaper and quieter, and it is potentially much faster, than a maglev train,” said Rob Enderle, an analyst with Enderle Group who studies Silicon Valley technology.“从理论上讲，与磁悬浮列车相比，超级高铁成本更低、噪音更小，且速度可能更快，”恩德勒集团（Enderle Group）研究硅谷技术的分析师罗布·恩德勒（Rob Enderle）表示。

2. Maglev Trains 磁悬浮列车

2. Maglev Trains 磁悬浮列车

“Magnetic levitation” trains are not just a lofty dream held over from the 1950s. They are already in operation in Shanghai and Japan. South Korea is building a maglev train that will operate within the Incheon Airport, and China reportedly has a second maglev train in development.
“磁悬浮”列车并非20世纪50年代遗留下来的空想。它们已在上海和日本投入运营。韩国正在建造一条将在仁川机场内部运行的磁悬浮列车，据报道，中国还有第二条磁悬浮列车正在开发中。
A magnetic force lifts and propels (提升和推进)the train using a minimal amount of energy compared to diesel-powered or electric-powered trains. The trains whisk passengers along at up to 310 miles per hour. A planned maglev train will transport passengers over 200 miles between Nagoya and Tokyo in just 40 minutes, helping to free congested roads, reduce air pollution, and reduce accidents.与柴油动力或电力驱动的列车相比，磁力列车使用最少的能量即可实现列车的提升和推进。列车以高达每小时310英里的速度载着乘客疾驰而过。计划中的磁浮列车将在短短40分钟内将乘客从名古屋运送到东京，全程超过200英里，这将有助于缓解道路拥堵，减少空气污染，并降低事故发生率。
   Of course, the main issue with maglev trains is the high cost of development. Because of the fast speeds, the trains have to be routed directly between destinations.当然，磁悬浮列车的主要问题是开发成本高昂。由于速度极快，列车必须直接在目的地之间运行。

3. Autonomous Vehicles 自动驾驶汽车

，超级高铁成本更低、噪音更小，且速度可能更快，”恩德勒集团（Enderle Group）研究硅谷技术的分析师罗布·恩德勒（Rob Enderle）表示。

2. Maglev Trains 磁悬浮列车

“Magnetic levitation” trains are not just a lofty dream held over from the 1950s. They are already in operation in Shanghai and Japan. South Korea is building a maglev train that will operate within the Incheon Airport, and China reportedly has a second maglev train in development.
“磁悬浮”列车并非20世纪50年代遗留下来的空想。它们已在上海和日本投入运营。韩国正在建造一条将在仁川机场内部运行的磁悬浮列车，据报道，中国还有第二条磁悬浮列车正在开发中。
A magnetic force lifts and propels (提升和推进)the train using a minimal amount of energy compared to diesel-powered or electric-powered trains. The trains whisk passengers along at up to 310 miles per hour. A planned maglev train will transport passengers over 200 miles between Nagoya and Tokyo in just 40 minutes, helping to free congested roads, reduce air pollution, and reduce accidents.与柴油动力或电力驱动的列车相比，磁力列车使用最少的能量即可实现列车的提升和推进。列车以高达每小时310英里的速度载着乘客疾驰而过。计划中的磁浮列车将在短短40分钟内将乘客从名古屋运送到东京，全程超过200英里，这将有助于缓解道路拥堵，减少空气污染，并降低事故发生率。
   Of course, the main issue with maglev trains is the high cost of development. Because of the fast speeds, the trains have to be routed directly between destinations.当然，磁悬浮列车的主要问题是开发成本高昂。由于速度极快，列车必须直接在目的地之间运行。

3. Autonomous Vehicles 自动驾驶汽车

content:
A robotic driver can think faster and smarter than a human driver—and look in all directions at once. That’s the idea behind autonomous driving, where you take your hands off the wheel and let the car do the driving for you. Ford has already announced a project called Traffic Jam Assist and Cadillac is working on something called Super Cruise that lets the car take over.(get control of it) 机器人驾驶员/司机能比人类驾驶员/司机反应更快、更智能，还能同时观察各个方向。这就是自动驾驶背后的理念，让你无需手握方向盘，让汽车为你驾驶。福特已经宣布了一个名为“交通拥堵辅助”的项目，而凯迪拉克正在研发一种名为“超级巡航”的技术，可以让汽车接管驾驶任务。
     Still, Google is leading the charge. It now uses a fleet of about 24 Lexus RX450h vehicles that have logged a total of about 500,000 miles on California roads. The cars can look for exit ramps, detect buildings, stop suddenly for other cars and change speeds as needed.尽管如此，谷歌正在引领潮流。目前，谷歌拥有约24辆雷克萨斯RX450h汽车，这些汽车在加利福尼亚州的道路上总共行驶了约50万英里。这些汽车能够寻找出口匝道、识别建筑物、为其他车辆突然刹车，并根据需要改变速度。
      Enderle says there are many prototypes already on the road, especially those being tested in San Francisco. Nevada has already created laws that make them legal to use in cities, including Las Vegas. In fact, Enderle says autonomous driving could appear within two years if it weren’t for some nagging legal issues (such as how to insure them) and public safety concerns.恩德勒表示，目前已有许多原型车在道路上行驶，尤其是在旧金山进行测试的车辆。内华达州已制定法律，规定这些车辆在城市（包括拉斯维加斯）合法使用。事实上，恩德勒表示，如果不是因为一些棘手的法律问题（如如何为它们投保）和公共安全问题，自动驾驶汽车可能会在两年内问世。
Smart Cars智能汽车 One way to solve transportation problems in major cities is to make the cars much smaller and smarter. So-called “smart cars” have been around for many years. But there are signs of progress. Many automakers, including BMW and Nissan, already offer compact electric cars. The BMW i3, already available in Europe, can brake automatically when you take your foot off the accelerator, consumes no gasoline and operates for 80–100 miles per charge.
解决大城市交通问题的一种方法是让汽车变得更小、更智能。所谓的“智能汽车”已经存在多年。但有迹象表明，相关技术正在取得进展。包括宝马和日产在内的许多汽车制造商已经推出了紧凑型电动汽车。宝马i3已在欧洲上市，当你松开油门时，它会自动刹车，无需消耗汽油，每次充电可行驶80至100英里。
   “I do believe that there is a growing opportunity for new types of vehicles specifically designed for urban areas,” Koslowski said, adding that these cars need more of a “wow” factor and will have to become part of an urban area’s overall plan for better transportation in a city, not just showy small cars for individual drivers.科斯洛夫斯基（Koslowski）表示：“我确实认为，专为城市地区设计的新型车辆将有越来越多的机会。”他补充说，这些汽车需要更具“惊艳”的特质，并且必须成为城市整体交通规划的一部分，以改善城市交通状况，而不仅仅是供个人驾驶的炫酷小型汽车。

：“我确实认为，专为城市地区设计的新型车辆将有越来越多的机会。”他补充说，这些汽车需要更具“惊艳”的特质，并且必须成为城市整体交通规划的一部分，以改善城市交通状况，而不仅仅是供个人驾驶的炫酷小型汽车。

5. Urban Transport Pods 城市交通舱

What if you could jump into a moving pod and speed away to another part of the city? That is what the Milton Keynes neighborhood about 45 miles northwest of London is planning. The pods seat one person and move on their own over a pre-described route.如果可以跳进一个移动舱，快速前往城市的另一个区域，会怎样？位于伦敦西北约45英里处的米尔顿·凯恩斯社区正计划实现这一设想。这种舱体仅容纳一人，可沿预设路线自动行驶。
The idea is that the human operator interacts with the pod using a touchscreen in the windshield. You swipe to select a destination, and you can read the daily news, check your e-mail or even play a video game during the trip. There will be a built-in wireless hotspot to connect your gadgets. The pod operates on its own, showing its current route.其理念是，乘客通过挡风玻璃上的触摸屏与舱体互动：轻滑选择目的地，旅途中可阅读每日新闻、查看邮件甚至玩电子游戏。舱内配备内置无线热点，供电子设备连接。舱体完全自动运行，实时显示当前路线。
   Similar pods are already being used in Masdar City in Abu Dhabi and at the London Heathrow Airport, but both are used in tightly controlled areas.类似舱体已在阿布扎比的马斯达尔城和伦敦希思罗机场投入使用，但均限于严格管控的区域。
Jon Beasley, the program director

tightly controlled areas.类似舱体已在阿布扎比的马斯达尔城和伦敦希思罗机场投入使用，但均限于严格管控的区域。

at Transport Systems Catapult who is charged with developing the technology, says the project is an “urban laboratory” where they can test not just the autonomous pods but also how they work in a real public setting.负责技术开发的“交通系统弹射器”项目主管乔恩·比斯利表示，该项目是一个“城市实验室”，不仅可以测试自动驾驶舱体，还能观察其在真实公共场景中的运行情况。
“We want to gain familiarity with future transport solutions in one area, to make it easier for industrial collaborators to come together and work together,” he says. (999 words)“我们希望在一个领域熟悉未来的交通解决方案，以便工业合作者更容易聚集在一起并协同工作，”他说道。
Unit Eight Smart Farming 智慧农业 Text A Smart Farming: The Future of Agriculture “Smart farming”is an emerging concept that refers to managing farms using technologies like IoT, robotics, drones and AI to increase the quantity and quality of products while optimizing the human labor required by production.智能农业：农业的未来 “智能农业”是一个新兴概念，指的是利用物联网、机器人技术、无人机和人工智能等技术来管理农场，以提高产品的数量和质量，同时优化生产所需的人力。

需的人力。
The Internet of Things has provided ways to improve nearly every industry imaginable. In agric

ulture, IoT has not only provided solutions to often time-consuming and tedious tasks but is totally changing the way we think about agriculture. What exactly is a smart farm, though? Here is a rundown of what smart farming is and how it’s changing agriculture.物联网为几乎所有能想到的行业提供了改进途径。在农业领域，物联网不仅为那些往往耗时且繁琐的任务提供了解决方案，而且正在彻底改变我们对农业的看法。那么，智能农场究竟是什么呢？下面简要介绍一下智能农业及其对农业的变革。
What Is a Smart Farm?
      A smart farming refers to managing farms using modern information and communication technologies to increase the quantity and quality of products while optimizing the human labor required.什么是智能农场？
      智慧农业是指利用现代信息和通信技术来管理农场，以提高产品的数量和质量，同时优化所需的人力劳动。
Among the technologies available for present-day farmers are:当今农民可用的技术包括：
● Sensors: soil, water, light, humidity, temperature management传感器：土壤、水分、光照、湿度、温度管理 ● Software:  specialized software solutions that target specific farm types or application-agnostic IoT platforms 软件：针对特定农场类型或与应用程序无关的物联网平台的专用软件解决方案 ● Connectivity: cellular, LoRa 连接性：蜂窝式网络系统、低功耗

局域网无线标准 ● Location: GPS, satellites 位置：GPS，卫星 ● Robotics: autonomous tractors, processing facilities机器人技术：自动驾驶拖拉机、加工设施 ● Data analytics: standalone analytics solutions, data pipelines for downstream solutions数据分析：独立的分析解决方案、下游解决方案的数据管道 Armed with such tools, farmers can monitor field conditions and make strategic decisions for the whole farm or a single plant without even needing to step foot in the field.有了这些工具，农民无需亲临田间/踏入田地，就能监测田间状况，并为整个农场或单株植物做出战略决策。
The driving force of smart farming is IoT —connecting machines and sensors integrated on farms to make farming processes data-driven and automated.智能农业的驱动力是物联网——将农场中集成的机器和传感器连接起来，使农业流程数据化并实现自动化。
The IoT-Based Smart Farming Cycle基于物联网的智能农业循环 The core of IoT is the data you can draw from things and transmit over the Internet. To optimize the farming process, IoT devices installed on a farm should collect and process data in a repetitive cycle that enables farmers to react quickly to emerging issues and changes in ambient conditions. Smart

farming follows a cycle like this one:物联网的核心在于，你可以从各种设备中提取数据，并通过互联网进行传输。为了优化农业流程，安装在农场上的物联网设备应在一个重复的周期内收集和处理数据，使农民能够快速应对新出现的问题和环境条件的变化。智能农业遵循这样一个循环周期：

1. Observation. Sensors record observational data from crops, livestock, soil, or the atmosphere.观测。传感器记录来自作物、牲畜、土壤或大气的观测数据。
2. Diagnostics. The sensor values are fed to a cloud-hosted IoT platform with predefined decision rules and models—also called “business logic”—that ascertain the condition of the examined object and identify any deficiencies or needs. 诊断。传感器值被输入到云端托管的物联网平台，该平台配备有预定义的决策规则和模型（也称为“业务逻辑”），用于确定被检查对象的状况，并识别任何缺陷或需求。
3. Decisions. After issues are revealed, the user, and/or machine learning-driven components of the IoT platform determine whether location-specific treatment is necessary and if so, which. 决策。问题暴露后，用户和/或物联网平台中的机器学习驱动组件将判断是否需要针对性地进行处理，若需要，则确定具体处理方式。
4. Action. After end-user evaluation and action, the cycle repeats from the beginning.行动。在最终用户评估和采取行动后，循环从头开始重复。

IoT Solutions to Agricultural Problems物联网解决农业问题 Many believe that IoT can add value to all areas of farming, from growing crops to forestry. While there are several ways that IoT can improve farming, two of the major ways IoT can revolutionize agriculture are precision farming and farming automation.许多人认为，物联网（IoT）可以为农业的各个领域，从种植作物到林业，增添价值。虽然物联网可以通过多种方式改善农业，但其中两大能够彻底改变农业的方式是精准农业和农业自动化。

1. Precision Farming1. 精准农业

IoT Solutions to Agricultural Problems物联网解决农业问题 Many believe that IoT can add value to all areas of farming, from growing crops to forestry. While there are several ways that IoT can improve farming, two of the major ways IoT can revolutionize agriculture are precision farming and farming automation.许多人认为，物联网（IoT）可以为农业的各个领域，从种植作物到林业，增添价值。虽然物联网可以通过多种方式改善农业，但其中两大能够彻底改变农业的方式是精准农业和农业自动化。

1. Precision Farming1. 精准农业

content:
可以为农业的各个领域，从种植作物到林业，增添价值。虽然物联网可以通过多种方式改善农业，但其中两大能够彻底改变农业的方式是精准农业和农业自动化。

1. Precision Farming1. 精准农业

Precision farming, or precision agriculture, is an umbrella concept for IoT-based approaches that make farming more controlled and accurate. In simple words, plants and cattle get precisely the treatment they need, determined by machines with superhuman accuracy. The biggest difference from the classical approach is that precision farming allows decisions to be made per square meter or even per plant/animal rather than for a field.精准农业是基于物联网的方法的总括性概念，这些方法使农业更具可控性和精确性。简而言之，植物和牲畜会得到由具有超人精度的机器所确定的精准的照疗。与传统方法最大的不同在于，精准农业允许根据每平方米甚至每株植物/每只动物的情况来做出决策，而不是针对整个田地。
By precisely measuring variations within a field, farmers can boost the effectiveness of pesticides and fertilizers, or use them selectively.通过精确测量田地内的变化，农民可以提高农药和化肥的效用，或选择性地使用它们。As is the case of precision agriculture, smart farming techniques enable farmers to better monitor the needs of individual animals and to adjust their nutrition accordingly, thereby preventing disease and enhancing herd health.与精准农业一样，智能养殖技术使农民能够更好地监测每只动物的需求，并据此调整其营养摄入，从而预防疾病并提升畜群健康。
Large farm owners can use wireless IoT applications to monitor the location, well-being, and health of their cattle. With this information, they can identify sick animals, so that they can be separated from the herd to prevent the spread of disease.
大型农场主可利用无线物联网应用来监控牛群的位置、状态与健康状况。通过这些信息，他们可以识别出生病的动物，从而将其与牛群分开，以防止疾病的传播。

2. Automation in Smart Greenhouses智能温室中的自动化

2. Automation in Smart Greenhouses智能温室中的自动化

content:
Traditional greenhouses control the environmental parameters through manual intervention or a proportional control mechanism, which often results in production loss, energy loss, and increased labor cost. 传统温室通过人工干预或比例控制机制来调节环境参数，这往往会导致生产损失、能源损失以及劳动力成本的增加。
IoT-driven smart greenhouses can intelligently monitor as well as control the climate, eliminating the need for manual intervention. Various sensors are deployed to measure the environmental parameters according to the specific requirements of the crop. That data is stored on a cloud-based platform for further processing and control with minimal manual intervention.物联网驱动的智能温室能够智能地监测和控制气候，无需人工干预。根据作物的特定需求，部署了各种传感器来测量环境参数。这些数据存储在基于云的平台中，以便在最少人工干预的情况下进行进一步处理和控制。
Agriculture is one of the major verticals to incorporate both ground-based and aerial drones for crop health assessment, irrigation, crop monitoring, crop spraying, planting, soil and field analysis, and other spheres/field/sector.农业是主要应用领域之一，该领域将地面无人机和空中无人机相结合，用于作物健康评估、灌溉、作物监测、作物喷洒、种植、土壤和田间分析以及其他领域。
Since drones collect multispectral, thermal, and visual imagery while flying, the data they gather provide farmers with insights into a whole array of metrics: plant health indices, plant counting and yield prediction, plant height measurement, canopy cover mapping, field water pond mapping, scouting reports, stockpile measuring, chlorophyll measurement, nitrogen content in wheat, drainage mapping, weed pressure mapping, and so on.由于无人机在飞行过程中会收集多光谱、热像和视觉图像，它们所收集的数据为农民提供了对一系列指标的详细信息：植物健康指数、植株计数和产量预测、植物高度测量、冠层覆盖度测绘、田间积水测绘、侦察报告、库存测量、叶绿素测量、小麦中的氮含量、排水测绘/水系测绘、杂草压力测绘等等。
Importantly, IoT-based smart farming doesn’t only target large-scale farming operations; it can add value to emerging trends in agriculture like organic farming, family farming, including breeding particular cattle and/or growing specific cultures,(the raising of plants or animals) preservation of particular or high-quality varieties, and enhance highly transparent farming to consumers, society and market consciousness.重要的是，基于物联网的智能农业不仅针对大规模农业经营；它还能为农业中的新兴趋势增添价值，如有机农业、家庭农业，包括饲养特定牛种和/或种植特定作物、保存特定或优质品种，以及增强对消费者、社会和市场的高度透明农业意识。
What’s Next in Smart Farming智慧农业的下一步是什么 Of course, all of these innovations are useless if they are not providing solutions to global problems. Here are two ways that smart farming is going to impact （influencing strongly） the future for the better.当然，如果这些创新不能为全球问题提供解决方案，那么它们就毫无用处。以下是智能农业将使未来变得更好的两种方式。

content:
fluencing strongly） the future for the better.当然，如果这些创新不能为全球问题提供解决方案，那么它们就毫无用处。以下是智能农业将使未来变得更好的两种方式。

1. Internet of Food, or Farm 2020 食品互联网，或2020年农场

If we have the Internet of Things (IoT) and the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), why not have one for food? The European Commission project Internet of Food and Farm 2020 (IoF 2020), a part of Horizon 2020 Industrial Leadership, explores through research and regular conferences the potential of IoT technologies for the European food and farming industry.如果我们有物联网（IoT）和医疗物联网（IoMT），为何不建立一个食品物联网呢？欧盟委员会的“食品与农业物联网2020”（IoF 2020）项目是“地平线2020工业领导力”计划的一部分，该项目通过研究和定期会议，探索物联网技术在欧洲食品和农业领域的潜力。
IoT has fostered the belief that a smart network of sensors, actuators, cameras, robots, drones, and other connected devices will bring an unprecedented level of control and automated decision-making to agriculture, making possible an enduring ecosystem of innovation in this eldest of industries.物联网（IoT）催生了一种信念，即由传感器、执行器、摄像头、机器人、无人机和其他互联设备构成的智能网络将为农业带来前所未有的控制水平和自动化决策能力，使这一历史悠久的行业得以构建一个持久的创新生态系统。

2. Third Green Revolution第三次绿色革命

2. Third Green Revolution第三次绿色革命

content:
人机和其他互联设备构成的智能网络将为农业带来前所未有的控制水平和自动化决策能力，使这一历史悠久的行业得以构建一个持久的创新生态系统。

2. Third Green Revolution第三次绿色革命

Smart farming and IoT-driven agriculture are paving the way for what can be called a Third Green Revolution.智能农业和物联网驱动的农业正在为所谓的第三次绿色革命铺平道路。
Following the plant breeding and genetics revolutions, the Third Green Revolution is taking over agriculture. That revolution draws upon the combined application of data-driven analytics technologies, such as precision farming equipment, IoT, big data analytics, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), robotics, etc.继植物育种和遗传学革命之后，第三次绿色革命正在席卷农业领域。这场革命依赖于数据驱动分析技术的综合应用，如精准农业设备、物联网、大数据分析、无人机（UAVs）、机器人技术等。
In the future, as this smart farming revolution depicts, pesticide and fertilizer use will drop while overall efficiency will rise. IoT technologies will enable better food traceability, which in turn will lead to increased food safety. It will also be beneficial for the environment, through, for example, more efficient use of water, or optimization of treatments and inputs.未来，正如这场智能农业革命所描绘的，农药和化肥的使用量将下降，而整体效率将提高。物联网技术将使食品的可追溯性得到提升，从而增强食品安全。此外，通过更高效地利用水资源或优化处理和投入，还将对环境产生益处。
Therefore, smart farming has a real potential to deliver a more productive and sustainable form of agricultural production, based on a more precise and resource-efficient approach. New farms will finally realize the eternal dream of mankind. It’ll feed our population, which may explode to 9.6 billion by 2050. 因此，智能农业凭借其更精确且资源高效的方法，具有实现更高产、更可持续农业生产的真正潜力。新型农场将最终实现人类的永恒梦想。它将养活我们的人口，到2050年，全球人口可能会激增至96亿。

It will also be beneficial for the environment, through, for example, more efficient use of water, or optimization of treatments and inputs.未来，正如这场智能农业革命所描绘的，农药和化肥的使用量将下降，而整体效率将提高。物联网技术将使食品的可追溯性得到提升，从而增强食品安全。此外，通过更高效地利用水资源或优化处理和投入，还将对环境产生益处。
Therefore, smart farming has a real potential to deliver a more productive and sustainable form of agricultural production, based on a more precise and resource-efficient approach. New farms will finally realize the eternal dream of mankind. It’ll feed our population, which may explode to 9.6 billion by 2050. 因此，智能农业凭借其更精确且资源高效的方法，具有实现更高产、更可持续农业生产的真正潜力。新型农场将最终实现人类的永恒梦想。它将养活我们的人口，到2050年，全球人口可能会激增至96亿。
